## iter 448 Рђћ New viz: why L1 gives sparsity (the geometry) (visualizations)
Broke a 5-iteration second-dd streak with a new visualization (the catalog gap I found: biasРђЊvariance is covered by `dl-overfitting` +
`ps-estimator`, but the **L1-sparsity geometry** was not). Pairs with the L1-vs-L2 dd from iter 431.
- **New viz `dl-regularization-geometry`** (viz 88 Рєњ **89**) Рђћ "Why L1 gives sparsity (the geometry)". Weight-space picture: gold loss
  contours around the unconstrained optimum `w*`, the constraint region at the origin (L1 diamond or L2 circle), and the regularized
  **solution = projection of `w*` onto the region**. Sliders for `wРѓЂ*, wРѓѓ*, t` + an **L1РєћL2 toggle**. With L1 the contour snaps to a
  **corner** (a weight becomes exactly 0 Рєњ the note flags "Sparse!"); with L2 (a circle, no corners) both weights only shrink. Drag
  `w*` toward an axis to trigger sparsity. Projection math (exact L1/L2 projection) node-verified before building.
- Embedded in `dl-overfitting-and-regularization`; bumped the `viz-complete` achievement target to 89.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **headless** lab route renders (errs=0, canvas), default L1 `w*=(3,1),t=2` Рєњ solution `(2.00, 0.00)` flagged
"Sparse!", L1РєћL2 toggle flips the note correctly; screenshot eyeballed (clean weight-space geometry). SW cache `atlas-v387` Рєњ `atlas-v388`.

## iter 447 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across PS/DL/algo (deep-dives 206 Рєњ **209**; 59 lessons now carry two):
- **ps-law-of-large-numbers** (had: "LLN vs CLT") Рєњ **the gambler's fallacy**: independent trials have no memory Рђћ the average
  converges by *dilution* (later data swamps early flukes), not compensation; weak (in probability) vs strong (almost sure) LLN.
- **dl-gans** (had: "a two-player game") Рєњ **mode collapse**: fooling D per-sample РЅа covering the distribution, so the generator can
  perfect a narrow slice; minibatch discrimination / Wasserstein / feature matching push back toward diversity.
- **a-graph-representations-traversal** (had: "queue vs stack") Рєњ **adjacency list vs matrix**: matrix `O(V┬▓)` space / `O(1)` lookup vs
  list `O(V+E)` / `O(deg)` Рђћ density decides (sparse Рєњ list, the default; dense or edge-query-heavy Рєњ matrix).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** each lesson opens **both** dd's (nDD=2) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke (2 dd's ├Ќ 3 +
pages) **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. SW cache `atlas-v386` Рєњ `atlas-v387`.

## iter 446 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across algo/calc/LLM (deep-dives 203 Рєњ **206**; 56 lessons now carry two):
- **a-comparison-sorts** (had: "quicksort vs mergesort") Рєњ **the ╬Е(n log n) lower bound**: a decision tree must separate all `n!` orderings,
  so height `РЅЦ logРѓѓ(n!) = ╬ў(n log n)` Рђћ `n log n` is a *theorem*, and counting/radix sorts only beat it by not comparing.
- **c-linearization-lhopital** (had: "why L'H├┤pital works") Рєњ **linearization**: `f(x)РЅѕf(a)+fРђ▓(a)(xРѕњa)` (e.g. `Рѕџ4.1РЅѕ2.025`) Рђћ the local
  model behind Newton's method, error propagation, `sin╬ИРЅѕ╬И`, and every gradient-descent step.
- **l-pretraining-objective-data** (had: "scaling laws") Рєњ **why next-token prediction learns everything**: predicting text *forces*
  grammar/facts/arithmetic/reasoning (each lowers the loss) Рђћ a self-supervised, massively-multitask objective with free labels.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.

## iter 445 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across PS/LA/DL (deep-dives 200 Рєњ **203**; 53 lessons now carry two):
- **ps-covariance-correlation** (had: "covariance with units divided out") Рєњ **correlation only sees lines (and isn't causation)**:
  `r=0` РЅа independent (`y=x┬▓` over symmetric `x` has `r=0` despite perfect dependence), and `r` is direction-blind to causation.
- **la-inverse-and-systems** (had: "you almost never compute the inverse") Рєњ **when `Ax=b` has a (unique) solution**: invertible
  (`detРЅа0`, full rank) Рєњ exactly one for every `b`; singular splits into no solution (b outside col space) or infinitely many.
- **dl-convolution-operation** (had: "weight-sharing + locality") Рєњ **stride/padding/output-size**: `Рїі(WРѕњK+2P)/SРїІ+1` (same-pad
  `32Рєњ32`, stride-2 `32Рєњ16`); stacking `3├Ќ3` kernels grows the receptive field (`5├Ќ5`, `7├Ќ7`) cheaply.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.

## iter 444 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives Рђћ the 200th (content / depth Рђћ owner's #1)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across calc/DL/RL, crossing **200 deep-dives** (197 Рєњ **200**; 50
lessons now carry two):
- **c-optimization** (had: "РѕЄf=0 is necessary, not sufficient") Рєњ **the closed-interval method**: on `[a,b]` the extremum can be at an
  *endpoint* (where `fРђ▓РЅа0`) Рђћ e.g. `f(x)=x` on `[0,1]` has no interior critical point; complete optimization checks the boundary too.
- **dl-embeddings-and-tokenization** (had: "symbols into geometry") Рєњ **why tokenization fumbles spelling/digits**: the model sees
  *subword* tokens, never raw letters Рђћ hence "how many r's in strawberry?", brittle arithmetic, and number-splitting quirks.
- **rl-monte-carlo** (had: "MC vs TD") Рєњ **first-visit vs every-visit MC**: average returns after the first vs every visit (both converge),
  but MC needs the *full* return so it updates only at episode end Рђћ can't handle continuing tasks (the limitation TD removes).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.

## iter 443 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across LA/algo/PS (deep-dives 194 Рєњ **197**; 47 lessons now carry two):
- **la-dot-product-norms** (had: "measures alignment") Рєњ **CauchyРђЊSchwarz**: `|a┬иb| РЅц РђќaРђќРђќbРђќ`, so `cos╬И = a┬иb/(РђќaРђќРђќbРђќ) Рѕѕ [Рѕњ1,1]` Рђћ the
  bound that makes cosine similarity a trustworthy, magnitude-free score (the engine of embedding/retrieval search).
- **a-np-completeness** (had: "they stand or fall together") Рєњ **P vs NP**: NP = easy to *verify*, P = easy to *find*; the open question
  is whether verifying-easy implies finding-easy Рђћ and NP-complete problems are where it concentrates.
- **ps-uniform-exponential** (had: "the exponential forgets") Рєњ **exponential РЄё Poisson**: in a Poisson process at rate `╬╗`, counts per
  interval are Poisson and the *gaps* are Exponential(╬╗) (mean `1/╬╗`) Рђћ two faces of one process.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set (now incl. a zero-width-char check) + dup-summary guard.

## iter 442 Рђћ Three more worked examples (LLM); UI/UX audited as mature (examples)
Intended a UI/UX iteration per the owner's steer, but audited first and found the obvious wins **already in place**: lesson keyboard
nav (`[`/`]`), РїўK search *inside lessons*, reading-progress bar + back-to-top, clickable glossary (439) & Library refs, the Copy button
(440), and a "?" shortcuts overlay. Rather than manufacture low-value UI churn, continued the content north star with **examples**
(varies from 441's dd; draws down the LLM pool). A **3rd worked example** on three LLM lessons (examples 440 Рєњ **443**):
- **l-positional-encoding** Рђћ **RoPE**: rotating q,k by `m╬Ирхб` makes the score `РЪеR_m q, R_n kРЪЕ = РЪеq, R_{nРѕњm}kРЪЕ` depend only on the
  *relative* offset `nРѕњm` Рђћ why rotary encodings generalize to longer contexts.
- **l-prompting-and-in-context-learning** Рђћ **prompt brittleness**: the model conditions on the *literal* tokens, so phrasing/order/format
  are all knobs Рђћ prompting steers a distribution, it doesn't issue a command.
- **l-safety-and-frontier** Рђћ **why prompt injection is stubborn**: instructions and data share one token stream with no privilege
  separation, so untrusted content can always try to act like instructions Рђћ defense is depth + least privilege, not one filter.
Injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard; caught & stripped a stray zero-width space before commit.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**443 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** all 3 reveal with KaTeX (79 / 33 / 17 spans), **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;
smoke + pages **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. SW cache `atlas-v381` Рєњ `atlas-v382`.

## iter 441 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across LA/PS/LLM (deep-dives 191 Рєњ **194**; 44 lessons now carry two):
- **la-basis-dimension** (had: "a basis is a coordinate system you choose") Рєњ **change of basis**: same arrow, new coordinates Рђћ
  `x = P[c]_B`, `[c]_B = PРЂ╗┬╣x` (`[3,1]_B Рєњ (4,2)` and back); the engine behind eigendecomposition/PCA picking the *special* basis.
- **ps-bernoulli-binomial** (had: "where the formula comes from") Рєњ **mean `np`, variance `np(1Рѕњp)`**, maximized at `p=┬й` (a fair coin
  is the most unpredictable; `n=10` Рєњ var 2.5 at 0.5 vs 0.9 at 0.1/0.9) Рђћ why proportions near 50% need the largest sample.
- **l-embeddings-and-prediction-head** (had: "the head is a similarity search") Рєњ **weight tying**: input embedding `E` and output
  head `Eрхђ` are the same map run both ways; sharing it halves a huge `V├Ќd` (~51M at 50k├Ќ1024), regularizes, and improves perplexity.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.

## iter 438 Рђћ Three more worked examples; RL at-2 pool cleared (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three lessons (examples 437 Рєњ **440**); this empties the reinforcement-learning course's 2-example backlog
(only LLM lessons now remain at 2):
- **rl-connections-frontiers** Рђћ **TD error = dopamine**: the reward-prediction error `╬┤=r+╬│V(sРђ▓)РѕњV(s)` matches dopamine-neuron firing
  (Schultz) Рђћ the burst shifts from reward to predicting cue; RL became a computational theory of biological reward learning.
- **l-multihead-and-causal-masking** Рђћ **what multiple heads buy**: one head averages one relationship; splitting `d` into `h` heads
  (`512Рєњ8├Ќ64`) attends to `h` patterns in parallel subspaces for the same `d├Ќd` cost Рђћ heads, not width, are the unit of attention capacity.
- **l-optimization-and-stability** Рђћ **why Adam, not SGD**: Adam's per-parameter step `m/(Рѕџv+╬х)` adapts to a transformer's wildly
  different gradient scales; AdamW adds decoupled weight decay Рђћ per-coordinate adaptation is why it trains stably out of the box.
Injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard.

## iter 437 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across algo/DL/PS (deep-dives 188 Рєњ **191**; 41 lessons now carry two):
- **a-shortest-paths-topo** (had: "why Dijkstra needs non-negative edges") Рєњ **Bellman-Ford**: relax every edge `VРѕњ1` times (`O(VE)`)
  to handle negative weights, and a `V`-th relaxing pass flags a negative cycle Рђћ exhaustive DP where Dijkstra's greedy assumption fails.
- **dl-the-artificial-neuron-and-mlp** (had: "neuron=logreg, MLP=universal") Рєњ **universal approximation's catch**: "can represent" РЅа
  "efficiently/learnably" Рђћ shallow nets may need exponentially many units; depth composes features for exponentially compact, learnable representations.
- **ps-poisson** (had: "the law of rare events") Рєњ **the binomial limit**: `Poisson(╬╗)=lim Binomial(n,╬╗/n)` (`Bin(1000,0.005)РЅѕPoisson(5)`),
  and the signature `mean=variance=╬╗` doubles as an overdispersion diagnostic.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** each lesson opens **both** dd's (nDD=2) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke (2 dd's ├Ќ 3 +

## iter 436 Рђћ Three more worked examples; DL at-2 pool cleared (examples)
First confirmed the live deploy is healthy after the owner's CNAME revert: `index.html` HTTP 200, title intact, **sw.js serving
`atlas-v375`** (iter-435 deploy landed), no local CNAME. Then a **3rd worked example** on three lessons (examples 434 Рєњ **437**); this
empties the deep-learning course's 2-example backlog:
- **dl-pretraining-and-finetuning-paradigm** Рђћ **the compute split**: `CРЅѕ6ND` gives pretraining `~3.2e23` FLOPs vs a `1e7`-token finetune
  `~1.1e19` Рђћ **~30,000├Ќ** more (finetune РЅѕ 0.003%); the economic case for foundation models (pay once, adapt cheaply forever).
- **l-hallucination-and-evaluation** Рђћ **why LLMs hallucinate**: next-token training rewards *fluency, not truth*, with no built-in
  "I don't know" Рђћ structural, so fixes wrap the model (RAG, tools, calibration/abstention, verification) rather than patch weights.
- **rl-offline** Рђћ **stay in distribution**: overestimation comes from querying `Q` on out-of-data actions; the fix family is policy
  constraint (BCQ) or value penalty (CQL) Рђћ a conservatism dial between overestimating and merely imitating.
Every value node-verified (30,000├Ќ); injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard (cost amounts written as words to avoid a stray money `$`).

## iter 435 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across DL/algo/RL (deep-dives 185 Рєњ **188**; 38 lessons now carry two):
- **dl-activation-functions** (had: "without nonlinearity depth is an illusion") Рєњ **the dying ReLU**: the *derivative* shape decides
  trainability Рђћ sigmoid/tanh saturate (`¤ЃРђ▓Рєњ0`), ReLU dies for `z<0` (0 output, 0 gradient); Leaky/GELU keep gradient flowing.
- **a-hash-tables** (had: "O(1) by turning keys into addresses") Рєњ **collisions & load factor**: `O(1)` is *expected/amortized* Рђћ
  chains grow with `╬▒=n/m`, so tables double at `╬▒РЅѕ0.75`; a bad hash/adversary forces `O(n)`.
- **rl-dqn** (had: "two tricks fix the two failure modes") Рєњ **maximization bias & Double DQN**: `max` over noisy Q's overestimates;
  Double DQN selects with the online net, evaluates with the target net, decorrelating the lucky overestimate.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.

## iter 434 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/RL/LLM (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons (examples 431 Рєњ **434**):
- **dl-transfer-learning** Рђћ **why finetune with a small LR**: a large LR overwrites the pretrained features (catastrophic forgetting),
  so you use `~2e-5` vs `1e-3`, plus discriminative LRs (tiny for early layers) and warmup Рђћ adapt, don't retrain.
- **rl-trpo-ppo** Рђћ **the importance ratio**: `r = ¤ђ_╬И/¤ђ_old` reweights old-policy samples for the new policy, letting PPO take *several*
  gradient steps per batch; the clip `[1Рѕњ╬х,1+╬х]` (e.g. caps `r=1.5` at `1.2`) keeps the off-policy reuse honest Рђћ the source of PPO's sample efficiency.
- **l-inference-efficiency** Рђћ **prefill vs decode**: prefill processes the whole prompt in one parallel, compute-bound pass (`O(n┬▓)` attention, once);
  decode is one forward pass per token, memory-bandwidth-bound Рђћ which is why KV-cache, batching, and quantization target decode.
Every value node-verified (r=1.5Рєњ1.2); injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**434 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** all 3 reveal with KaTeX (35 / 107 / 70 spans), **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;
smoke + pages **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. SW cache `atlas-v373` Рєњ `atlas-v374`.

## iter 433 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across LA/LLM/algo (deep-dives 182 Рєњ **185**; 35 lessons now carry two):
- **la-matrix-multiplication** (had: "is function composition") Рєњ **the cost of multiplying**: `mnp` mults per product, and
  associativity is free but cost isn't Рђћ `(AB)C`=7,500 vs `A(BC)`=75,000 flops (10├Ќ) for the same result; the matrix-chain/contraction-order problem.
- **l-decoding-strategies** (had: "three knobs on one trade-off") Рєњ **why not greedy/beam**: maximizing likelihood gives bland, repetitive
  text (the likelihood trap) Рђћ "best text" РЅа "most likely text", so open-ended generation samples; beam is for closed tasks (translation/code).
- **a-trees-heaps** (had: "heap vs BST invariants") Рєњ **why a BST must stay balanced**: `O(log n)` is really the height; sorted inserts
  collapse a plain BST into an `O(n)` linked list, so AVL/red-black rotations keep height `~log n` for a worst-case guarantee.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** each lesson opens **both** dd's (nDD=2) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke (2 dd's ├Ќ 3 +

## iter 432 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/RL/LLM (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons (examples 428 Рєњ **431**):
- **dl-initialization-and-vanishing-gradients** Рђћ **why you can't init weights to zero**: identical weights get identical gradients
  (`РѕѓL/РѕѓwРѓЂ=РѕѓL/РѕѓwРѓѓ`) and stay identical forever Рђћ the *symmetry problem*; init must both break symmetry (randomness) and set scale (Xavier/He).
- **rl-practical-rl** Рђћ **policy-invariant reward shaping**: only the potential-based form `F=╬│╬д(sРђ▓)Рѕњ╬д(s)` leaves the optimal policy
  unchanged (the terms telescope), so you can speed learning with hints without corrupting the objective.
- **l-finetuning-and-instruction-tuning** Рђћ **instruction tuning elicits, doesn't teach**: ~1,000 curated examples (LIMA) suffice
  because SFT unlocks latent ability and the chat *format*, not new knowledge Рђћ pretraining learns, SFT aligns.
Injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard; the `&amp;` in a citation renders correctly (no double-encoding).

## iter 431 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across DL/PS/RL (deep-dives 179 Рєњ **182**; 32 lessons now carry two):
- **dl-overfitting-and-regularization** (had: "a vote for simpler explanations") Рєњ **L1 vs L2**: L2's gradient `2╬╗wрхб` shrinks smoothly
  (dense), L1's constant `╬╗┬иsign(wрхб)` drives weights *exactly* to 0 (sparse); the L1 diamond's axis-corners are why lasso selects features.
- **ps-expectation-variance** (had: "expectation always adds; variance is pickier") Рєњ **why variance squares**: sign-cancellation,
  differentiability, and additivity for independents (`Var(X+Y)=Var(X)+Var(Y)`) Рђћ at the cost of squared units, which `¤Ѓ=РѕџVar` undoes.
- **rl-mdp-formalism** (had: "the Markov property") Рєњ **the discount factor ╬│**: makes the infinite return converge (`╬Б╬│рхЈ=1/(1Рѕњ╬│)`),
  encodes preference-for-sooner, and sets an effective horizon `РЅѕ1/(1Рѕњ╬│)` (╬│=0.99 Рєњ ~100 steps).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** each lesson opens **both** dd's (nDD=2) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke (2 dd's ├Ќ 3 +

## iter 430 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/RL/LLM (examples)
Resuming the examplesРєћdeep-dives rotation after the backprop deep-focus. A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons
(examples 425 Рєњ **428**):
- **dl-ml-recap-and-the-learning-problem** Рђћ **biasРђЊvariance decomposition**: `E[(yРѕњf╠ѓ)┬▓] = Bias┬▓ + Var + ¤Ѓ┬▓`; a rigid line
  (`4+1+1=6`) can beat a flexible polynomial (`0.5+6+1=7.5`) Рђћ total error is U-shaped in complexity.
- **rl-sarsa-qlearning** Рђћ **Expected SARSA**: sample (SARSA), max (Q-learning), or policy-average the next-state value;
  `╬Б¤ђ(a|sРђ▓)Q = 0.9┬и10+0.1┬и2 = 9.2` Рђћ same mean as SARSA, lower variance; greedy ¤ђ recovers Q-learning.
- **l-transformer-block** Рђћ **where parameters live**: attention is `4d┬▓` (1.05M at d=512), FFN is `8d┬▓` (2.10M) Рђћ the FFN holds
  ~**67%** of a block's weights (and much of its knowledge), which is why MoE/PEFT target it.
Every value node-verified (6 vs 7.5; 9.2; 67%); injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard.

## iter 429 Рђћ Backprop in matrix form: РѕѓL/РѕѓW = ╬┤aрхђ (content / depth Рђћ owner's backprop focus)
Backprop follow-up #3 (owner's deep-focus), completing the depth arc. Added a **third** deep-dive to `dl-backpropagation` (it now
carries 3; total deep-dives Рєњ **179**) Рђћ distinct from its existing two ("why backprop goes backward", "product of Jacobians"):
- **"the matrix form Рђћ backprop is two matmuls per layer"**: for a layer `z=Wa+b`, given the upstream error `╬┤=РѕѓL/Рѕѓz`, the gradients
  are `РѕѓL/РѕѓW = ╬┤aрхђ` (outer product, same shape as `W`), `РѕѓL/Рѕѓb = ╬┤`, `РѕѓL/Рѕѓa = Wрхђ╬┤`. Shows how the *shapes force the formulas*
  (`РѕѓL/РѕѓW_ij = ╬┤рхбaР▒╝`), why forward uses `W` and backward uses `Wрхђ`, and how **batching** becomes one matmul `РѕѓL/РѕѓW = ╬ћAрхђ` Рђћ so each
  layer's backward pass is two matmuls (`Wрхђ╬ћ`, `╬ћAрхђ`), the same order as the forward pass. Numerically consistent with the
  `la-matrix-calculus-backprop` example (`Wрхђ╬┤=[2,3]`).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX (a `$$Рђд$$` display block); injected via the append-dd path with the full guard set + dup-summary guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** the lesson opens **all 3** dd's (nDD=3, 48 KaTeX spans) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**. SW
cache `atlas-v368` Рєњ `atlas-v369`. *(Backprop focus now spans: 2 viz + 4 examples + code exercise + 3 deep-dives across
dl-backpropagation, plus c-chain-rule and la-matrix-calculus-backprop.)*

## iter 426 Рђћ Step-through backpropagation visualization (visualizations Рђћ owner's request)
**Owner asked to go deep on backpropagation** (examples, visualizations). Audited existing coverage first: backprop is taught across
`dl-backpropagation` (2 dd + the single-weight `dl-backprop` viz), `c-chain-rule`, `la-matrix-calculus-backprop`,

chain rule compose. So I built a new flagship widget.
- **New viz `dl-backprop-graph`** (viz 87 Рєњ **88**) Рђћ "Backprop step-by-step (with an activation)". A real 2-layer chain
  `x Рєњ┬иwРѓЂРєњ zРѓЂ Рєњ¤ЃРєњ aРѓЂ Рєњ┬иwРѓѓРєњ ┼и Рєњ (┼иРѕњy)┬▓ Рєњ L` drawn as a computational graph. **РЌђ Prev / Next РќХ / Reset** walk the **forward pass**
  (each node value lights up gold), then the **backward pass** node-by-node (each gradient in rust). Every backward step narrates the
  chain rule as *upstream ├Ќ local derivative* Рђћ including the activation step `РѕѓL/РѕѓzРѓЂ = РѕѓL/РѕѓaРѓЂ ┬и ¤ЃРђ▓(zРѓЂ)`, the factor beginners miss.
  A **¤Ѓ Рєћ ReLU toggle** shows how the activation's slope changes the flow (¤ЃРђ▓РЅц0.25 shrinks Рєњ vanishing gradients; ReLUРђ▓Рѕѕ{0,1} gates Рєњ
  dead units). Sliders for `x, wРѓЂ, wРѓѓ, y` make every number hand-checkable.
- Embedded in `dl-backpropagation` before the deep-dives with a lead-in; bumped the `viz-complete` achievement target to 88.

## iter 425 Рђћ Three more worked examples; algo at-2 pool cleared (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three lessons (examples 421 Рєњ **424**); this empties the algorithms course's 2-example backlog:
- **a-amortized-analysis** Рђћ **the binary counter**: `n` increments cost `<2n` bit-flips total (potential `╬д`=#1-bits Рєњ amortized 2),
  so `O(1)` amortized despite a worst-case `O(k)` single increment.
- **a-union-find-range** Рђћ **the inverse Ackermann**: union-by-rank + path compression give `O(╬▒(n))`; `╬▒(n)РЅц4` for any conceivable
  `n`, so "practically constant" (either optimization alone is only `O(log n)`).
- **rl-value-approximation** Рђћ **the deadly triad**: function approximation + bootstrapping + off-policy can diverge (Baird), though
  any two are safe Рђћ why DQN uses a target network + replay.
Every value node-verified (8Рєњ15 flips, 1000Рєњ1994РЅѕ2n; ╬▒ facts; triad); injected byte-stably with the round-trip guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**424 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** all 3 reveal with KaTeX (56 / 38 / 66 spans) and **kErr=0**; the new

## iter 424 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth Рђћ owner's #1)
A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across DL/PS/LA (deep-dives 175 Рєњ **178**; 30 lessons now carry two):
- **dl-attention-mechanism** (had: "soft differentiable dictionary lookup") Рєњ **where Q,K,V come from**: all linear projections of the
  input (`Q=XW_Q`Рђд); self-attention takes them from one sequence, cross-attention queries another Рђћ one primitive, two wirings.
- **ps-confidence-intervals** (had: "what 95% confidence means") Рєњ **what sets the width**: half-width `z┬и¤Ѓ/Рѕџn` Рђћ the Рѕџn law (4├Ќ data to
  halve it) and the confidenceРєћwidth tradeoff (`zРѓЅРѓЁ=1.96` vs `zРѓЅРѓЅ=2.576`).
- **la-symmetric-spectral** (had: "stretch along perpendicular axes") Рєњ **why ML is full of symmetric matrices**: covariance, Gram/kernel,
  and Hessian are symmetric *by construction* (`AрхђA`, mixed partials) Рђћ which is exactly what PCA, kernels, and 2nd-order optimization need.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + duplicate-summary guard.

## iter 423 Рђћ Three more worked examples across RL/DL/algo (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across RL/DL/algo (examples 418 Рєњ **421**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **rl-actor-critic** Рђћ **why a baseline doesn't bias the gradient**: `E_a[РѕЄlog ¤ђ ┬и b] = b┬иРѕЄ╬Б¤ђ = 0`, so subtracting any
  action-independent `b` keeps the mean but cuts variance; the optimal `b=V(s)` gives the advantage `A=QРѕњV` Рђћ what the critic learns.
- **dl-diffusion-models** Рђћ **the forward process is free**: noising is a fixed Gaussian schedule (closed form
  `x_t=РѕџрЙ▒_t xРѓђ+Рѕџ(1РѕњрЙ▒_t)╬х`); only the reverse is learned, as MSE **noise prediction** `Рђќ╬хРѕњ╬х_╬ИРђќ┬▓` Рђћ why diffusion trains so stably.
- **a-network-flow** Рђћ **bipartite matching as max flow**: unit-capacity `sРєњworkersРєњjobsРєњt`; by the integrality theorem max-flow =
  max matching Рђћ a modeling hammer (disjoint paths, project selection, segmentation all reduce to flow).
Every value node-verified (baseline expectation РЅѕ0; diffusion variance; reduction); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons across calc/algo/LLM (deep-dives 172 Рєњ **175**; 27 lessons now carry two):
- **c-convexity** (had: "local minimum is global") Рєњ **how to recognize/build convex functions**: the Hessian-PSD test (`f''РЅЦ0`) plus the
  convex calculus (nonneg sums, pointwise max, affine composition) Рђћ why `РђќAxРѕњbРђќ┬▓+╬╗РђќxРђќРѓЂ` is provably convex.
- **a-divide-and-conquer** (had: "balanced splits") Рєњ **the Master Theorem**: compare `f(n)` to `n^{log_b a}` Рђћ mergesort/binary search
  are the tie case, Karatsuba (`logРѓѓ3РЅѕ1.585`) is leaf-dominated. "Which level of the tree holds most of the work?"
- **l-scaling-laws** (had: "the Chinchilla correction") Рєњ **what scaling laws can't predict**: smooth loss can hide *emergent* skills,
  and a power-law fit is only evidence within its range Рђћ a trend, not a guarantee.

A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons (examples 415 Рєњ **418**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **l-rag-and-tools** Рђћ **the retrieval context budget**: a `4000`-token window minus `500` overhead fits `Рїі3500/500РїІ=7` chunks;
  chunk size is a precisionРєћcoverage dial coupled to `k` and the window.
- **dl-transformer-architecture** Рђћ **why attention divides by РѕџdРѓќ**: `q┬иk` of unit-variance vectors has variance `dРѓќ` (std `Рѕџ64=8`),
  saturating softmax into a hard argmax with vanishing gradients; `/РѕџdРѓќ` restores stdРЅѕ1, keeping logits in the sensitive range.
- **dl-autoencoders-vae** Рђћ **the reparameterization trick**: `z=╬╝+¤Ѓ╬х`, `╬хРѕ╝N(0,1)` moves randomness to a parameter-free input so
  `Рѕѓz/Рѕѓ╬╝=1`, `Рѕѓz/Рѕѓ¤Ѓ=╬х` and gradients flow Рђћ the score-function obstacle solved oppositely (differentiate the sample, not the probability).

## iter 419 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/RL/algo (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across DL/RL/algo (examples 412 Рєњ **415**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **dl-learning-rate-schedules-and-tuning** Рђћ **the LR stability ceiling**: for `f=x┬▓`, GD `xРєљ(1Рѕњ2╬и)x` converges iff `0<╬и<1` (╬и=0.5 Рєњ
  one step; ╬и>1 diverges); generally `╬и<2/L` Рђћ set by the largest curvature, which is why LR is tuned first and warmup/decay exist.
- **rl-policy-gradient-theorem** Рђћ **the score-function trick**: `РѕЄE[R] = E[R┬иРѕЄlog ¤ђ]` via `РѕЄ¤ђ = ¤ђРѕЄlog ¤ђ` Рђћ you can't differentiate
  through a sample, but you can differentiate the probability that produced it; "gradient of an expectation Рєњ expectation of a gradient."
- **a-arrays-lists-stacks-queues** Рђћ **array vs linked list**: complementary Big-O (`O(1)` access vs `O(1)` insert), but cache locality
  decides real speed Рђћ contiguous arrays prefetch, list pointer-chasing misses, so Big-O is necessary but not sufficient.
Every value node-verified (╬и threshold; log-derivative identity; Big-O table); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons (deep-dives 166 Рєњ **169**; 21 lessons now carry two):
- **dl-backpropagation** (had: "why backprop goes backward") Рєњ **vanishing/exploding gradients as a product of Jacobians**: depth
  multiplies per-layer factors `cРЂ┐` (`0.9РЂхРЂ░РЅѕ0.005`, `1.1РЂхРЂ░РЅѕ117`) Рђћ init, normalization, residuals, and clipping all attack this product.
- **rl-value-iteration** (had: "Bellman operator is a contraction") Рєњ **value iteration vs policy iteration**: VI = *truncated* PI Рђћ
  one backup/sweep (cheap, many, asymptotic at rate ╬│) vs full evaluation (costly, few, exact). A dial on how much you evaluate before improving.
- **ps-normal-distribution** (had: "why the normal is everywhere") Рєњ **standardization & the 68-95-99.7 rule**: the z-score collapses
  every normal onto one curve; 3¤Ѓ (`z=3`) is ~1-in-740 per tail Рђћ "how unusual" becomes a single universal number.

## iter 417 Рђћ Three more worked examples across LLM/LA/calc (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across LLM/LA/calc (examples 409 Рєњ **412**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **l-what-is-a-language-model** Рђћ **perplexity**: `PPL = exp(avg NLL)`; for `p=[0.5,0.25,0.5]` it's `e^0.924 РЅѕ 2.52` Рђћ the effective
  branching factor (cross-entropy in "number of choices" units).
- **la-low-rank-pca** Рђћ **why PCA needs centering**: PCA diagonalizes the *covariance* (defined about the mean); skip centering and
  the top direction is dragged toward the centroid (where the data *is*, not how it *varies*).
- **c-lagrange-multipliers** Рђћ **╬╗ is a shadow price**: for `max xy s.t. x+y=S`, `╬╗=S/2=df*/dS` (envelope theorem) Рђћ the marginal
  value of relaxing the constraint, the same idea behind sensitivity analysis and regularization strength.
Every value node-verified (2.52; centering contrast; ╬╗=5=df*/dS); injected byte-stably with the full guard set. (LA & calc are now

- **a-binary-search** (had: "the invariant & its bugs") Рєњ **binary search on the answer**: it needs only a *monotone predicate*, not a
  sorted array Рђћ `Рѕџx`, first-bad-version, and feasibility problems all bisect the parameter (`O(log range)` checks).
- **dl-pooling-and-cnn-architectures** (had: "pooling buys invariance") Рєњ **convolution is weight sharing**: one `3├Ќ3` kernel = `9`
  weights at any image size vs a dense layer's `~10РЂХ`, encoding locality + translation equivariance as architecture.
- **ps-conditional-independence-bayes** (had: "natural frequencies") Рєњ **what naive Bayes assumes**: conditional independence
  factorizes the likelihood (`P(c|x) РѕЮ P(c)РѕЈP(xрхб|c)`); usually false, yet classifies well because only the argmax matters.

## iter 415 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/PS/RL (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across DL/PS/RL (examples 406 Рєњ **409**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **dl-gans** Рђћ **the GAN equilibrium**: optimal `D*=p_data/(p_data+p_g)`; when `p_g=p_data`, `D=0.5` everywhere and `V=Рѕњlog4РЅѕРѕњ1.386` Рђћ
  success is a stalemate, which is why GANs lack a single decreasing loss to watch.
- **ps-conditional-expectation** Рђћ **E[X|Y] from a joint table**: slice the `Y=0` column, renormalize, average Рєњ `E[X|Y=0]=0.75`
  (vs marginal `E[X]=0.7`); "slice, renormalize, average."
- **rl-policy-iteration** Рђћ **why it terminates fast**: strictly-improving over `|A|^|S|` finite policies (`2┬│=8` here), so it reaches
  the optimum in finitely many steps Рђћ a monotone climb, unlike value iteration's asymptotic convergence.
Every value node-verified (Рѕњlog4; 0.75; РЅц8); injected byte-stably with the full guard set (now incl. a stray-`<p>` check).

## iter 413 Рђћ Three more worked examples across algo/calc/LA (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across algo/calc/LA (examples 403 Рєњ **406**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **a-mst-union-find** Рђћ **the cut property**: the cheapest edge crossing any cut is in some MST (exchange argument) Рђћ the single
  theorem behind both Prim and Kruskal.
- **c-improper-integrals** Рђћ **a vertical-asymptote singularity**: `РѕФРѓђ┬╣ x^(Рѕњ1/2) dx = 2` converges despite blowing up at 0; the
  finite-endpoint p-test mirrors the tail one Рђћ converges iff `p<1`.
- **la-diagonalization** Рђћ **defective matrices**: `[[2,1],[0,2]]` has eigenvalue 2 (alg mult 2) but only a 1-D eigenspace, so it's
  *not* diagonalizable Рђћ geometric < algebraic multiplicity breaks `PD PРЂ╗┬╣`.
Every value node-verified (РѕФ=2; defective alg2/geom1; cut min edge); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

A **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons (deep-dives 157 Рєњ **160**; 12 lessons now carry two):
- **l-rlhf-and-preference-optimization** (had: "DPO drops the reward model") Рєњ **why RLHF needs a KL leash**: optimizing a *learned*
  reward invites reward hacking (Goodhart), so `E[r] Рѕњ ╬▓┬иD_KL(¤ђРђќ¤ђ_ref)` anchors the policy near the SFT model Рђћ the same objective DPO bakes in.
- **a-greedy** (had: "exchange argument") Рєњ **fractional vs 0/1 knapsack**: greedy-by-ratio is optimal when items are divisible, but
  0/1 strands capacity (cap 10: greedy takes a 6-item, B+C=10 wins) Рђћ divisibility/matroid structure is what licenses greedy.
- **la-determinants** (had: "det=0 РЄњ singular; det(AB)=detA┬иdetB") Рєњ **the determinant is signed volume-scaling**: every det fact
  follows from "oriented volume scaling" (collapse РЄњ 0, products multiply, triangular = diagonal product).

  checkpoints, swappable adapters, because the fine-tuning update is low-rank.
- **ps-hypothesis-testing-logic** Рђћ **one-sided vs two-sided**: `z=1.8` rejects at the one-sided cutoff `1.645` but fails the two-sided
  `1.96`; the cutoff depends on the hypothesis, which must be fixed before looking (else p-hacking).

## iter 409 Рђћ Three more worked examples Рђћ the 400th (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across calc/RL/LA, crossing **400 worked examples** (397 Рєњ **400**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **c-gradient-directional** Рђћ **directional derivative РіЦ gradient = 0**: at `(1,1)`, `РѕЄf=(2,2)`, moving along `(1,Рѕњ1)/Рѕџ2` gives `D_u f=0`
  Рђћ you're on the level curve; the gradient is always perpendicular to level sets.
- **rl-model-based** Рђћ **Dyna planning multiplier**: `100├Ќ(1+5)=600` value updates from 100 real steps; a learned model replays
  synthetic experience (data Рєњ compute efficiency), bounded by model bias.
- **la-matrix-calculus-backprop** Рђћ **two-layer backprop**: `РѕѓL/Рѕѓx = WРѓЂрхђWРѓѓрхђ(РѕѓL/Рѕѓz) = (2,3)` Рђћ multiply by transposes in reverse
  order; reverse-mode autodiff keeps every intermediate a vector, so backprop РЅѕ one forward pass.
Every value node-verified (D_u=0; 600; (2,3)); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**400 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** all 3 reveal with KaTeX (55 / 5 / 41 spans) and **kErr=0,
rawDollar=0**; smoke + 8 pages **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v348` Рєњ `atlas-v349`.

## iter 408 Рђћ Three more second deep-dives on the hardest lessons (content / depth Рђћ owner's #1)
Continuing the depth runway: a **distinct second "Deeper dive"** on three more flagship lessons (deep-dives 151 Рєњ **154**; 6 lessons
now carry two), each a different sub-concept from the existing one:
- **dl-transformer-architecture** (had: "mix then think") Рєњ **why stack identical blocks**: depth is repeated *refinement* of one
  residual stream Рђћ early blocks do syntax, late blocks semantics; same operation, more rounds (and why the residual is essential).
- **la-svd** (had: "rotate-stretch-rotate") Рєњ **EckartРђЊYoung**: truncating to the top `k` singular values gives the provably best
  rank-`k` approximation, error `╬Б_{i>k} ¤Ѓрхб┬▓` Рђћ the engine of PCA, compression, and LoRA.
- **c-gradient-descent-convergence** (had: "why GD zigzags / condition number") Рєњ **momentum smooths the zigzag**: `vРєљ╬▓v+РѕЄf`
  cancels the oscillating component and accumulates the consistent one, improving the rate's `╬║`-dependence toward `Рѕџ╬║`.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected via the append-second-dd path with the full guard set + duplicate-summary guard.
(Noted a harmless regex artifact: a `<summary>([^<]*)` scan reports 0 dd's when a summary contains inline `<em>` Рђћ coverage is intact
at 148/148; the dedup check uses a tag-tolerant match.)
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** each lesson opens **both** deep-dives (nDD=2) Рђћ la-svd KaTeX 26 with `\sum`/`\sigma`/`\gt`
Рђћ **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke (2 dd's ├Ќ 3 lessons + 8 pages) **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v347` Рєњ `atlas-v348`.

## iter 407 Рђћ Second deep-dives on three of the hardest lessons (content / depth Рђћ owner's #1)
Varying from the examples streak back to the owner's #1 directive (depth): added a **distinct second "Deeper dive"** to three
flagship lessons (deep-dives 148 Рєњ **151**; these lessons now carry two), each a different hard sub-concept from the existing one:
- **l-self-attention** (had: "soft dictionary lookup") Рєњ **why Q, K, V are separate projections**: raw-embedding scores would be
  symmetric (`xрхб┬иxР▒╝`), forcing reciprocal attention; separate `W_Q, W_K` break that, `W_V` decouples delivered content from match.
- **la-eigenvalues-eigenvectors** (had: "eigenvectors decide the long run") Рєњ **real eigenvalues stretch, complex ones rotate**:
  a real `╬╗` scales along its eigenvector; complex pairs `a┬▒bi` have no real eigenvector and rotate in a 2-D plane by `|╬╗|=Рѕџ(a┬▓+b┬▓)`.
- **dl-gradient-descent-and-optimizers** (had: "Adam = momentum + RMSProp") Рєњ **SGD's noise is a feature**: mini-batch jitter
  escapes saddles (`РѕЄf=0`) and biases toward flat, better-generalizing minima Рђћ an implicit regularizer.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with an *append-second-dd* path (round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +
unsupported-env + bare-`<`-letter + duplicate-summary guards).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** each lesson opens **both** deep-dives (nDD=2) Рђћ la-eigenvalues KaTeX 21 with
`\lt`/`\gt`/`\pm`/`\sqrt` Рђћ with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke opening 2 dd's ├Ќ 3 lessons + 8 pages **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. No
save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v346` Рєњ `atlas-v347`.

## iter 406 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/algo (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons (examples 394 Рєњ **397**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **dl-overfitting-and-regularization** Рђћ **L1 vs L2**: L2's penalty gradient `2╬╗w=0.02` fades as `wРєњ0` (shrinks, never zero); L1's
  `╬╗┬иsign(w)=0.1` is constant, so it pins weights to exactly 0 Рђћ sparsity / feature selection (the diamond's corners).
- **dl-practical-training-and-debugging** Рђћ **gradient checking**: central difference `(f(2.001)Рѕњf(1.999))/0.002 = 4.000` matches the
  analytic `f'(2)=4`; `O(h┬▓)` error verifies backprop before you trust it.
- **a-algorithms-for-ml** Рђћ **one k-means step**: assign `[1,2,9,10]` to centroids `0,8`, recompute means Рєњ `[1.5, 9.5]`;
  assign-average-repeat (Lloyd's) is coordinate descent to a local optimum.
Every value node-verified (4.000; L1 0.1 vs L2 0.02; centroids 1.5/9.5); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

  `¤ЃРЅѕ1.71`; the mean is a balance point, not an attainable face.

  matrix analogue of `d/dx(ax┬▓)=2ax`, the gradient behind every least-squares objective.

## iter 404 Рђћ Three more worked examples across calc/RL/algo (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across calc/RL/algo (examples 388 Рєњ **391**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **c-limits-intuition** Рђћ **the squeeze theorem**: `x┬▓sin(1/x) Рєњ 0`, trapped between `┬▒x┬▓` (both Рєњ 0); bound a wild function instead
  of evaluating it Рђћ the same trick behind `sin x / x Рєњ 1`.
- **rl-imitation** Рђћ **behavioral cloning compounds errors**: a 1% per-step error gives only `0.99┬╣РЂ░РЂ░ РЅѕ 0.366` on-track episodes;
  regret grows `O(T┬▓╬х)` because mistakes push the agent off the expert's distribution Рђћ DAgger relinearizes it.
- **a-correctness-invariants** Рђћ **a binary-search loop invariant**: "if present, the target is in `[lo, hi]`" Рђћ initialization /
  maintenance / termination proves correctness (a loop invariant is induction in disguise).

## iter 402 Рђћ Three more worked examples across LLM/DL/LA (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across LLM/DL/LA (examples 385 Рєњ **388**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **l-embeddings-and-prediction-head** Рђћ **weight tying**: input embedding + output head are each `V├Ќd = 25.6M` params at `V=50k, d=512`;
  sharing them (head = input embeddingрхђ) halves it to `25.6M` and adds an inductive bias.
- **dl-dropout-and-normalization** Рђћ **BatchNorm train vs test**: train normalizes per-batch, test uses frozen running stats
  (`╬╝_run Рєљ 0.9╬╝_run + 0.1╬╝_batch`) so inference is deterministic and works at batch size 1 Рђћ forgetting `model.eval()` is a classic bug.
- **la-orthonormal-gram-schmidt** Рђћ **Qрхђ = QРЂ╗┬╣**: for orthonormal `Q`, solving `Qx=(1,0)` is just `x=Qрхђ(1,0)=(0.6,Рѕњ0.8)` Рђћ inversion
  becomes transposition (`O(n┬▓)` not `O(n┬│)`), lengths preserved, condition number 1.
Every value node-verified (tying 25.6M; Qрхђb=(0.6,Рѕњ0.8)Рєњ(1,0); BN 0.4); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

## iter 399 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/RL/algo (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across DL/RL/algo (examples 379 Рєњ **382**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **dl-pooling-and-cnn-architectures** Рђћ **the conv output-size formula** `Рїі(WРѕњK+2P)/SРїІ+1`: `7,3,0,1 Рєњ 5`; `7,3,1,2 Рєњ 4` Рђћ
  padding preserves, stride downsamples, kernel shrinks; the three knobs that set a CNN's spatial geometry.
- **rl-monte-carlo** Рђћ **the discounted return** `GРѓђ = rРѓЂ+╬│rРѓѓ+╬│┬▓rРѓЃ = 2.62` (╬│=0.9): MC uses the *actual complete* return (no
  bootstrapping) Рђћ unbiased but high-variance, the mirror of TD.
- **a-backtracking-branch-bound** Рђћ **n-Queens solution count**: 4-Queens has exactly `2` solutions; backtracking prunes the
  `4РЂ┤=256` (or `4!=24`) space by abandoning a partial board the instant it conflicts.
Every value node-verified (conv 5/4; GРѓђ=2.62; 4-Queens=2); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

## iter 398 Рђћ Three more worked examples across calc/LA/PS (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across calc/LA/PS (examples 376 Рєњ **379**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **c-integration-techniques** Рђћ **definite integral by substitution**: `РѕФРѓђ┬╣ 2x e^{x┬▓} dx`, `u=x┬▓` converts the bounds (`0Рєњ1`), giving
  `РѕФРѓђ┬╣ eрхў du = eРѕњ1 РЅѕ 1.718`; change the limits and you never back-substitute.
- **la-dot-product-norms** Рђћ **CauchyРђЊSchwarz**: `|a┬иb| РЅц РђќaРђќРђќbРђќ` Рєњ `11 РЅц 11.18`, equality iff parallel; the algebraic shadow of
  `|cos ╬И| РЅц 1`, behind the triangle inequality and `Рѕњ1 РЅц ¤Ђ РЅц 1`.
- **ps-covariance-correlation** Рђћ **correlation is scale-free**: `¤Ђ = Cov/(¤ЃРѓЊ¤Ѓ_y) = 0.6`; rescale `YРєњ10Y` and covariance jumps
  `6Рєњ60` but `¤Ђ` stays `0.6` Рђћ units cancel.
Every value node-verified (eРѕњ1; 11РЅц11.18; ¤Ђ invariant); injected byte-stably with the full guard set.

## iter 396 Рђћ Three more worked examples across algo/RL/LLM (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across algo/RL/LLM (examples 373 Рєњ **376**, all РїўK-searchable), each a distinct
angle from the lesson's existing two (read first to avoid overlap):
- **a-shortest-paths-topo** Рђћ **why Dijkstra needs non-negative edges**: on `SРєњA(1), SРєњB(2), BРєњA(Рѕњ2)` the true dist to A is `0`
  (via B) but Dijkstra finalizes A at `1` and never revisits Рђћ the greedy "finalize and forget" rule needs Bellman-Ford when
  weights can be negative. (Complements the existing BF + critical-path examples.)
- **rl-what-is-rl** Рђћ **explore vs exploit (╬х-greedy)**: with `╬х=0.1, k=3`, `P(greedy)=1Рѕњ╬х+╬х/kРЅѕ0.933`, `P(other)=╬х/kРЅѕ0.033`;
  exploration is the price of learning from evaluative feedback.
- **l-self-attention** Рђћ **from scores to weights (the softmax step)**: scores `[2,1,0]` Рєњ softmax `[0.665,0.245,0.090]` Рєњ output
  `14.25`; the existing example was *given* the weights, this one derives them and explains why softmax (smooth, differentiable).

## iter 395 Рђћ Three more worked examples across LA/DL/PS (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across LA/DL/PS (examples 370 Рєњ **373**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **la-gradients-jacobians** Рђћ **the Jacobian stacks gradients**: for `F=(x┬▓y, x+y)`, `J(2,3)=[[12,4],[1,1]]`; it's the best linear
  approximation `F(p+╬┤)РЅѕF(p)+J╬┤`, and chaining maps multiplies Jacobians (the chain rule behind backprop).
- **dl-rnn-lstm-gru** Рђћ **why vanilla RNN gradients vanish/explode**: the multiplicative recurrence scales by `wрхђ` Рђћ `0.5┬╣РЂ░РЅѕ0.001`
  (vanish), `1.5┬╣РЂ░РЅѕ57.7` (explode); only `w=1` is stable, and the LSTM's *additive* cell-state highway fixes it.
- **ps-point-estimation** Рђћ **MLE for a coin is the sample frequency**: maximizing `log L = k log p + (nРѕњk)log(1Рѕњp)` gives `p╠ѓ=k/n`
  Рєњ `0.7` for 7/10; the binomial coefficient drops out.
Every value node-verified (J=[[12,4],[1,1]]; 0.5┬╣РЂ░/1.5┬╣РЂ░; MLE 0.7); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +
unsupported-env + bare-`<`-letter-in-math pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**373 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** all 3 reveal with KaTeX (41 / 58 / 62 spans Рђћ `bmatrix` + `\binom`
render clean) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; smoke + 8 pages **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v334` Рєњ `atlas-v335`.

## iter 394 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/PS/calc (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across DL/PS/calc (examples 367 Рєњ **370**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **dl-gradient-descent-and-optimizers** Рђћ **Adam's first step normalizes itself**: bias-corrected `m╠ѓ/Рѕџv╠ѓ = 1` regardless of the
  gradient's size (`= sign(g)` at t=1); Adam = momentum + RMSProp + bias correction, robust to badly-scaled losses.
- **ps-sampling-distributions** Рђћ **the 1/Рѕџn law**: `SE = ¤Ѓ/Рѕџn` Рєњ `2` at n=100, `1` at n=400; 4├Ќ the data halves the error
  (diminishing returns of precision).
- **c-gradient-descent-convergence** Рђћ **the learning-rate window**: GD on `┬йax┬▓` multiplies x by `(1Рѕњ╬иa)`, converging iff
  `0 < ╬и < 2/a` (=1 for a=2), fastest at `╬и=1/a` (one step); curvature bounds the safe rate.
Every value node-verified (Adam ratio 1; SE 2Рєњ1; threshold 2/a=1); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +
unsupported-env + bare-`<`-letter-in-math pre-guards (GD-convergence uses `\lt`/`\ge`).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**370 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** all 3 reveal with KaTeX (26 / 45 / 53 spans) and **kErr=0,
rawDollar=0**; smoke + 8 pages **errs=0/kErr=0, bad=none**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v333` Рєњ `atlas-v334`.

## iter 393 Рђћ Curve-sketching visualizer Рђћ 87th widget (visualizations)
`c-extrema-curve-sketching` had no widget. Added the **87th Lab widget `calc-curve-sketch`**, embedded there: the cubic
`f(x) = x┬│ Рѕњ a┬иx` drawn **green where fРђ▓ > 0** (rising) and **rust where fРђ▓ < 0** (falling), with gold dots at the **max** and
**min**. Slide **a**: the critical points `x = ┬▒Рѕџ(a/3)` slide toward each other (`a=3 Рєњ ┬▒1.00`) and at **a=0 merge and vanish** Рђћ
with no sign change in fРђ▓ there are no turning points, just an inflection. The "aha": the *sign* of the first derivative is the
graph's shape, and a turning point needs fРђ▓ to actually cross zero, not merely touch it. Plain-HTML note (no `$`). app.js
`viz-complete` fallback 86 Рєњ 87.

## iter 391 Рђћ Three more worked examples across DL/calc/algo (examples)

  off it; the decision boundary is the line `w┬иx+b=0` Рђћ one neuron draws only a *linear* split (why a single unit can't do XOR).
- **c-definite-integral-riemann** Рђћ **left/right/midpoint sums**: for `РѕФРѓђ┬▓x┬▓` (true `2.667`), `L=1`, `R=5`, `M=2.5`; midpoint's
  over/under-shoot cancels per strip, so its error shrinks as `O(1/n┬▓)` vs `O(1/n)`.
- **a-asymptotic-analysis** Рђћ **n log n vs n┬▓ at scale**: at `n=1000`, `РЅѕ9,966` vs `1,000,000` (100├Ќ); growth rate, not the
  constant, decides feasibility Рђћ the crossover Big-O captures.

  РіЦ to `a` Рђћ exactly why least squares projects `b` onto the column space (normal equations make the residual orthogonal).
- **ps-expectation-variance** Рђћ **Var = E[X┬▓]Рѕњ(E[X])┬▓**: for `{1,2,3}` with `[0.2,0.3,0.5]`, `E[X]=2.3`, `E[X┬▓]=5.9` Рєњ `Var=0.61`,
  `¤ЃРЅѕ0.781` Рђћ "mean of the square minus square of the mean," derived in one pass.
- **c-linearization-lhopital** Рђћ **linear approximation**: `Рѕџ4.1 РЅѕ L(4.1) = 2 + 0.25┬и0.1 = 2.025` (true `2.0248Рђд`); the error shrinks
  like `(xРѕњa)┬▓` Рђћ "differentiable" means "locally linear."
Every value node-verified (proj РіЦ residual; Var 0.61 / SD 0.781; 2.025 vs 2.0248); injected byte-stably with round-trip +

## iter 385 Рђћ Three more worked examples across calc/RL/LLM (examples)

- **c-partial-derivatives** Рђћ **the gradient points uphill (directional derivatives)**: `РѕЄf=(2,4)` at (1,2); rate toward `(3,4)` is
  `РѕЄf┬и├╗ = 4.4`; steepest ascent is `РѕЄf` itself at rate `|РѕЄf|=Рѕџ20РЅѕ4.47` Рђћ why gradient descent steps along `РѕњРѕЄf`.
- **rl-dqn** Рђћ **the DQN target**: `y = r + ╬│┬иmaxРѓљ Q(sРђ▓,a) = 1+0.9┬и5 = 5.5`; terminal drops the bootstrap (`y=r=1`); the `max`
  is what makes Q-learning off-policy.
- **l-decoding-strategies** Рђћ **top-p (nucleus) sampling**: grow the nucleus to cover `p=0.9` Рєњ keep `{0.5,0.25,0.15}`, renormalize
  to `[0.556,0.278,0.167]`; a dynamic cutoff that adapts to the model's confidence (unlike fixed top-k).
Every value node-verified (4.4 / 4.47; 5.5 / 1.0; nucleus renorm); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +

## iter 384 Рђћ РўЁ MILESTONE: every lesson now has a deeper-dive (148/148) (content / understandability)
The final 3 "Deeper dive" expandables Рђћ deep-dive coverage **145 Рєњ 148/148**. **Every lesson in the codex now carries a
deeper-dive**, completing the owner's #1 directive (depth / hard-concept extra explanations):
- **c-functions-and-graphs** Рђћ **a function is a machine, a graph is its fingerprint**: the single-valued rule, the vertical line
  test, and reading slope/roots/turning-points off the curve; formula transformations as rigid motions (`f(x)+c`, `f(x-c)`, `-f(x)`, `f(ax)`).
- **la-span-independence** Рђћ **independence is non-redundancy; span is reach**: the link via `cРѓЂvРѓЂ+Рђд+c_kv_k=0` (only trivial
  solution РЄњ independent); a basis as the balance point Рђћ maximal reach, zero redundancy.
- **la-matrix-derivative-identities** Рђћ **matrix calculus is the chain rule, bookkept by shapes**: the layout convention forces
  the result (`РѕЄРѓЊ(xрхђAx)=(A+Aрхђ)x` is the only vector that fits) Рђћ the same machinery backprop runs in reverse.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.

## iter 383 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives Рђћ deep-dive coverage now 145/148 (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across LLM/calc/LA/RL (deep-dives 141 Рєњ **145**; only **3 of 148** lessons left without one):
- **l-pretraining-objective-data** Рђћ **scaling laws**: test loss falls as a power law in params `N`, data `D`, compute `C`;
  Chinchilla's ~20-tokens-per-parameter compute-optimal split Рђћ capability is bought predictably, if you split compute right.
- **c-derivatives-special-functions** Рђћ **e╦Б is its own derivative**: the defining property of `e`; why `dy/dx=ky Рєњ Ce^{kx}` models
  all proportional growth, `ln` linearizes products, and the trig derivatives close a 4-cycle.
- **la-basis-dimension** Рђћ **a basis is a coordinate system you choose**: spanning + independent РЄњ unique coordinates; dimension is
  the invariant; most of applied LA is switching to the basis where the problem is easy (eigen/PCA/SVD).
- **rl-connections-frontiers** Рђћ **RL is the training signal behind aligned LLMs**: RLHF treats the LLM as a policy + a learned
  reward model (PPO); RL on verifiable rewards is how models are trained to reason Рђћ capability from pretraining, behaviour from RL.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.

## iter 382 Рђћ Intermediate Value Theorem visualizer Рђћ 84th widget (visualizations)
`c-continuity` had no widget. Added the **84th Lab widget `calc-ivt`**, embedded there: a continuous curve on `[a, b]` with a
**draggable target level k** (slider). The IVT guarantees that for any k between f(a) and f(b) there's a point c with f(c)=k Рђћ the
curve is marked (violet dot + drop-line) wherever it crosses k. A **"Add a jump" toggle** introduces a discontinuity: now a level
inside the gap has **0 crossings**, visibly breaking the theorem Рђћ continuity is exactly what forbids skipping a value. Plain-unicode
note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 83 Рєњ 84.

## iter 381 Рђћ Fix: streak (and all stat numbers) could display 0 instead of the real value (bug Рђћ owner report)
**Owner bug report:** the "N-day streak" display was rendering wrong. **Root cause:** the `countUp` animation (dashboard
stat-strip, Progress-page tiles) wrote `el.textContent = "0"` *synchronously*, then restored the real value only via a deferred
`setTimeout` + `requestAnimationFrame` chain. If that async path stalled Рђћ a backgrounded tab (rAF pauses), a slow device, or
navigating before the deferred timer fired Рђћ the number was **left stuck at "0"**. This explains the exact symptom: the header
streak counter (set directly in `renderChrome`, no animation) always showed the right value, while the **dashboard and Progress
"Day streak" (count-up'd) could show 0**. Seeded headless repro: with `streak=12`, header=12 but dashboard/stats="0" (and *every*
count-up number Рђћ Total XP, Cards, accuracy Рђћ was 0, confirming it's the shared count-up, not streak-specific).
**Fix (`js/app.js` `countUp`):**
- Moved the `"0"` zero-state *inside* `run()` so it's written only when the animation actually starts Рђћ a deferred-but-never-run
  element keeps its real value (and there's no pre-delay 0-flash).
- Added a **safety net**: a `setTimeout` after the animation window (`delay + dur + 260ms`) that forces the final real value if
  rAF hasn't landed it Рђћ so a stalled/throttled animation can never leave a number stuck at 0.
- Reduced-motion path unchanged (returns early, real value never zeroed).
Verified (seeded headless, `--dump-dom`): post-fix every count-up resolves to its real value Рђћ **Day streak 12** (was 0), Total XP
1,200, Cards 7, accuracy 90%. Across `streak = 1 / 12 / 100`: header, dashboard, the `cs-label` ("­ЪћЦ N-day streak Рђд"), and the
Progress tile all show the correct value with correct flame tiers (lit / hot / inferno). All-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0**; gate
ALL GREEN. SW cache `atlas-v320` Рєњ `atlas-v321` (app.js is a cached asset).

- **Structural coverage audit**: every one of 148 lessons has РЅЦ2 examples (**358**), РЅЦ8 MCQs (2368), РЅЦ1 flashcard (889),

**Reflection (371РђЊ379):** steady code/viz/content/examples rotation Рђћ all verified and pushed, no bugs; the gate hardening (330)
and kErr-audit discipline have held across 80 iterations since the 300 step-back. Deep-dive coverage is approaching completeness.
**The ship (content Рђћ toward "every lesson has a deeper-dive").** 4 more deep-dives (137 Рєњ **141**; only **7 of 148** left)
across RL/calc/LA/algo:
- **rl-practical-rl** Рђћ **why RL is notoriously hard**: a target that moves as the policy generates its own data; sparse/delayed
  rewards; the deadly triad Рђћ so practical RL is mostly stabilization machinery.
- **c-limits-intuition** Рђћ **a limit is about approach, not arrival**: it says nothing about `f(a)`; `sin x / x Рєњ 1` at 0 though
  `0/0` is undefined.
- **la-vectors-operations** Рђћ **a vector is both an arrow and a list**: geometry for intuition, components to compute Рђћ the duality
  that scales linear algebra to 768-dim data.
- **a-algorithms-for-ml** Рђћ **ML is optimization wrapped in linear algebra**: gradient descent + matmuls + the standard
  search/aggregation toolkit; no new kind of computation.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 0 / 20 / 1 / 1 spans) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;

## iter 379 Рђћ Three more code exercises across LA/DL/calc (new functionality / active learning)
Added **3 gate-verified JavaScript exercises** across LA/DL/calc (lessons-with-code 78 Рєњ **81**; the gate now runs **76**):
- **la-matrix-calculus-backprop** Рђћ **the weight gradient is an outer product** `dL/dW = (dL/dy)┬иxрхђ`: upstream `[1,2]`, input `[3,4]`
  Рєњ `3 4 6 8` (each `dL/dW_ij = dL/dy_i ┬и x_j`).
- **dl-autoencoders-vae** Рђћ **the reparameterization trick** `z = ╬╝ + ¤Ѓ┬и╬х`: `╬╝=2, ¤Ѓ=0.5, ╬х=1` Рєњ `2.50`; the noise sits outside the
  differentiable path so gradients flow.
- **c-implicit-related-rates** Рђћ **implicit differentiation** of `x┬▓+y┬▓=25` (`y'=Рѕњx/y`): at `(3,4)` Рєњ `-0.75`, the circle's slope.
Every output node-verified; injected byte-stably with round-trip + no-existing-`data-code` + `**`-free guards.

## iter 378 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across LA/calc/RL (examples 355 Рєњ **358**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **la-matrices-as-transformations** Рђћ **composition = matrix product**: scale-then-rotate is `RS=[[0,Рѕњ2],[2,0]]`, sending `(1,0)Рєњ(0,2)`;
  matrix multiply *is* function composition (right-to-left), which is why products don't commute.
- **c-multivariable-optimization** Рђћ **Lagrange multipliers**: maximize `xy` s.t. `x+y=10` via `РѕЄf=╬╗РѕЄg` Рєњ `x=y=5`, `f=25`; the
  optimum is where the gradient points straight out of the feasible set.
- **rl-eligibility-traces** Рђћ **the ╬╗-return blends every n-step return**: `G^╬╗=(1Рѕњ╬╗)GРѓЂ+╬╗GРѓѓ = 2` at `╬╗=0.5`; `╬╗=0` is TD(0),
  `╬╗=1` is Monte Carlo Рђћ the bias-variance dial.
Every value node-verified (RS┬и(1,0)=(0,2); xy=25; ╬╗-return 2); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +
unsupported-env pre-guards (bmatrix is KaTeX-supported).

## iter 377 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives Рђћ toward full coverage (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables on foundational lessons across RL/LLM/calc/PS (deep-dives 133 Рєњ **137**; only **11 of 148** lessons
now lack one):
- **rl-what-is-rl** Рђћ **RL learns from evaluation, not instruction**: a scalar reward, not the right answer Рђћ hence explore-vs-exploit
  and delayed credit assignment, which supervised learning never faces.
- **l-what-is-a-language-model** Рђћ **a next-token probability machine**: `P(next|context)`, applied autoregressively; competence
  *emerges* from doing that one thing well at scale.
- **c-area-volume** Рђћ **slice, approximate, integrate**: one template (`dx` strips, disks `¤ђr┬▓`, shells `2¤ђx┬иh`) behind every
  area/volume formula Рђћ set up one representative slice and integrate.
- **ps-geometric-waiting** Рђћ **the discrete memoryless wait**: the *only* discrete distribution with `P(X>m+n|X>m)=P(X>n)` Рђћ
  constant hazard, the gambler's fallacy made true for independent trials; the exponential's discrete twin.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.

## iter 376 Рђћ KV-cache visualizer Рђћ 83rd widget (visualizations)
`l-inference-efficiency` had no widget. Added the **83rd Lab widget `llm-kv-cache`**, embedded there: per-token generation work

(sage, reusing stored keys/values). Slide the **context length N** and the quadratic curve pulls away Рђћ over `N` tokens the
cumulative work is `╬Бt` (cached) vs `╬Бt┬▓` (uncached), a gap that grows with N (`7├Ќ at N=10 Рєњ 20├Ќ at 30 Рєњ 40├Ќ at 60`). The cache
is what makes long-context decoding feasible. Plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 82 Рєњ 83.

## iter 374 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across PS/algo/LA (examples 352 Рєњ **355**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **ps-law-of-large-numbers** Рђћ **LLN says it converges; CLT says how fast**: for n=100 the proportion `Рєњ0.5` (LLN) with SE
  `Рѕџ(0.25/100)=0.05` (CLT); LLN gives the limit, CLT the `1/Рѕџn` Gaussian wobble Рђћ both needed for error bars.
- **a-string-algorithms** Рђћ **why KMP beats naive**: "aaaa" in "aaaaaaaaaa" costs naive `(nРѕњm+1)┬иm = 28` (re-checking) vs KMP
  `O(n+m)=14` Рђћ the prefix function avoids re-reading text characters.
- **la-span-independence** Рђћ **do these span РёЮ┬│?**: `vРѓЃ=vРѓЂ+vРѓѓ` РЄњ dependent (`det=0`), so they span only the `xy`-plane, not `РёЮ┬│`;
  you need `n` independent vectors to span `РёЮРЂ┐`.
Every value node-verified (SE 0.05; 28 vs 14; det 0); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +
unsupported-env pre-guards.

## iter 373 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on foundational lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across LLM/calc/algo/PS (deep-dives 129 Рєњ **133**; only 15 of 148 lessons now lack one):
- **l-embeddings-and-prediction-head** Рђћ **the prediction head is a similarity search** (dot the hidden state against every
  token's embedding Рєњ softmax); often weight-tied to the input embedding.
- **c-differentiation-rules** Рђћ **rules = linearity + product/chain structure**: `(af+bg)'`, `(fg)'`, `(fРѕўg)'`, plus a few atoms,
  compose to differentiate anything Рђћ a mechanical descent through the expression tree.
- **a-linear-sorts-selection** Рђћ **beating n log n by not comparing**: counting/radix sort use keys as *array indices* (`O(n+k)`),
  sidestepping the comparison-sort lower bound Рђћ at the cost of needing bounded integer-like keys.
- **ps-errors-and-power** Рђћ **the two ways a test can be wrong**: Type I (`╬▒`, false alarm) vs Type II (`╬▓`, miss); lowering one
  raises the other at fixed `n` Рђћ which is worse is a domain choice (smoke alarm vs criminal trial).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX (`\lt` for the comparison sign); injected byte-stably with round-trip +
even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.

`l-pretraining-objective-data` (the next-token objective) had no widget. Added the **82nd Lab widget `llm-cross-entropy`**,
embedded there: a bar chart of the model's predicted distribution over 5 candidate tokens with the **true token highlighted**.
Slide the probability mass on the true token and the **cross-entropy loss `Рѕњlog(p)`** and **perplexity `e^loss = 1/p`** update
live Рђћ `p=0.2 Рєњ loss 1.61 / ppl 5`, `p=0.9 Рєњ loss 0.11 / ppl 1.11`. The "aha": the loss depends *only* on the mass on the true
token Рђћ confident-and-right costs almost nothing, confident-and-wrong (`pРєњ0`) is punished without bound. Plain-unicode note
(no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 81 Рєњ 82.

- **la-vectors-operations** Рђћ **distance = magnitude of the difference**: `РђќQРѕњPРђќ=Рђќ(3,4)Рђќ=5` Рђћ the norm is Pythagoras in any dimension.
- **a-approximation-randomized** Рђћ **randomized MAX-CUT**: each edge cut w.p. ┬й Рєњ `E[cut]=m/2=5 РЅЦ OPT/2`, a 2-approx from one
  coin flip per vertex (linearity of expectation, no independence needed).

## iter 369 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on foundational lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables on foundational lessons across algo/LA/calc/DL (deep-dives 125 Рєњ **129**):
- **a-trees-heaps** Рђћ **heap property vs BST property**: `left<node<right` buys searchability (`O(log n)` *if balanced*); a heap's
  parent-dominates-children buys only `O(1)` top-element access (priority queue) Рђћ different invariants, different jobs.
- **la-dot-product-norms** Рђћ **the dot product measures alignment**: `a┬иb = |a||b|cos ╬И` Рђћ sign gives direction, magnitude gives
  projection; normalized, it's cosine similarity. The bridge between algebra and geometry.
- **c-computing-limits** Рђћ **indeterminate forms are questions**: `x/xРєњ1`, `x┬▓/xРєњ0`, `x/x┬▓РєњРѕъ` are all "0/0" Рђћ the form doesn't
  determine the answer; what matters is the *rate* each part approaches its limit.
- **dl-ml-recap-and-the-learning-problem** Рђћ **learning minimizes an unseen expected loss**: you optimize empirical loss as a
  proxy for true risk; overfitting is the gap, and every ML technique keeps the proxy honest.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX (`\lt` for the angle/order signs); injected byte-stably with round-trip +
even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.

## iter 366 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across algo/calc/LA (examples 346 Рєњ **349**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **a-np-completeness** Рђћ **verify-vs-solve is the heart of NP**: a Subset-Sum certificate checks in `O(n)`, but brute force tries
  `2РЂ┐` subsets (`2РЂ┤РЂ░ РЅѕ 1.1├Ќ10┬╣┬▓`); P = solvable, NP = verifiable, and the gap is the open P-vs-NP question.
- **c-functions-and-graphs** Рђћ **composition shrinks the domain**: `(fРѕўg)(x)=Рѕџ(xРѕњ4)`, domain `[4,Рѕъ)`, `(fРѕўg)(8)=2`; the output of
  `g` must be a legal input to `f` Рђћ the same nesting the chain rule differentiates.
- **la-basis-dimension** Рђћ **why dimension is well-defined**: every basis of `РёЮ┬│` has exactly 3 vectors Рђћ 2 can't span, 4 must be
  dependent; a basis is the "Goldilocks" set, so dimension is unambiguous and coordinates unique.
Every value node-verified (`2РЂ┤РЂ░`; `(fРѕўg)(8)=2`; span/independence counts); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` +
tag-balance + unsupported-env pre-guards.

## iter 365 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on foundational lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables on foundational lessons across algo/LA/PS/calc (deep-dives 121 Рєњ **125**):
- **a-hash-tables** Рђћ **O(1) lookup by turning keys into addresses**: a hash function maps key Рєњ slot; collisions are inevitable
  (pigeonhole) so chaining/probing + the load factor `╬▒=n/m` (`O(1+╬▒)`) govern the speed-space tradeoff.
- **la-matrices-as-transformations** Рђћ **a matrix's columns are where the basis vectors land**: read the transformation off the
  columns; linearity does the rest Рђћ `[[0,Рѕњ1],[1,0]]` is a 90┬░ rotation.
- **ps-t-tests** Рђћ **why t, not z, for small samples**: plugging sample `s` for `¤Ѓ` adds uncertainty Рєњ heavier tails set by the
  degrees of freedom `nРѕњ1`; as `nРєњРѕъ`, t Рєњ normal. An honesty adjustment, not a rule.
- **c-derivative-definition** Рђћ **the derivative is a secant in the limit**: `f'(a)=lim (f(a+h)Рѕњf(a))/h`; the secant pivots to
  the tangent as `hРєњ0`, resolving the "instantaneous rate" paradox without dividing by zero.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 6 / 11 / 13 / 10 spans) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;
all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v304` Рєњ `atlas-v305`.

## iter 364 Рђћ PPO clipped-surrogate visualizer Рђћ 80th widget (visualizations)
`rl-trpo-ppo` had no widget. Added the **80th Lab widget `rl-ppo-clip`**, embedded there: PPO's objective
`min(r┬иA, clip(r,1Рѕњ╬х,1+╬х)┬иA)` plotted against the policy ratio `r` (╬х=0.2), with the raw `r┬иA` line faint behind it and the
clip boundaries marked. Toggle the **advantage sign**: for a good action (`A>0`) the objective rises then **flattens at r=1.2**
(capping the update); for a bad action (`A<0`) it's **floored at r=0.8** but keeps falling past r=1.2, **unclipped** Рђћ the
deliberate asymmetry that still pushes hard to undo a mistake. Plain-unicode/entity note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete`
fallback 79 Рєњ **80**.

## iter 362 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across algo/calc/PS (examples 343 Рєњ **346**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **a-recurrences-master-theorem** Рђћ **Case 2 (mergesort)**: `T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n)`, `a=b^d` (2=2┬╣) Рєњ work spreads evenly across
  `log n` levels Рєњ `╬ў(n log n)` Рђћ the balanced regime behind every optimal comparison sort.
- **c-continuity** Рђћ **a jump discontinuity can't be patched**: a piecewise `f` with left-limit `1 РЅа` right-limit `3` Рђћ the
  two-sided limit doesn't exist, so no `f(1)` value fixes it (vs a removable hole, which one redefinition mends).
- **ps-p-values** Рђћ **multiple comparisons**: 20 tests at `╬▒=0.05`, all nulls true Рєњ `1Рѕњ0.95┬▓РЂ░ РЅѕ 0.642`, a 64% chance of a
  false "discovery"; the engine of p-hacking, why Bonferroni (`╬▒/m`) exists.
Every value node-verified (Case 2 a=b^d; `1РЅа3` jump; `0.642`); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +

## iter 361 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on foundational lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across DL/RL/LA/calc (deep-dives 117 Рєњ **121**):
- **dl-pretraining-and-finetuning-paradigm** Рђћ **pretrain once, adapt cheaply**: generic features are amortized across tasks, so
  fine-tuning needs far less data/compute Рђћ representations transfer (the foundation-model recipe).
- **rl-mdp-formalism** Рђћ **the Markov property makes RL tractable**: the present state being a sufficient statistic is what lets
  `V(s)` depend on `s` alone and the Bellman equations exist; bad RL is often a non-Markov state.
- **la-diagonalization** Рђћ **`A^k = PD^kPРЂ╗┬╣` makes powers trivial**: in the eigenvector basis a matrix is a diagonal scaling, so
  `A^k v = ╬Б╬╗рхбрхЈcрхбvрхб` Рђћ largest `|╬╗|` dominates (PageRank, steady states, stability).
- **c-fundamental-theorem** Рђћ **the two halves of calculus are inverses**: `d/dx РѕФРѓљ╦Бf = f` and `РѕФРѓљрхЄf = F(b)РѕњF(a)` Рђћ accumulating
  a rate recovers the total, turning an infinite-sum into an algebra problem.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 0 / 3 / 17 / 4 spans Рђћ pretraining is intentionally
prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v300` Рєњ `atlas-v301`.

## iter 358 Рђћ Chain-rule visualizer Рђћ 78th widget (visualizations)
`c-chain-rule` had no widget Рђћ and it's the foundation of backprop. Added the **78th Lab widget `calc-chain`**, embedded
there: the curve `y = sin┬▓x = f(g(x))` with `g(x)=sin x`, `f(u)=u┬▓`. Slide **x** and the gold tangent's slope `dy/dx` is shown
to equal the **product of the rates** Рђћ the inner rate `gРђ▓=cos x` times the outer rate `fРђ▓(g)=2┬иsin x` Рђћ live, with both
factors and the product matching the slope. The "aha": a nudge in x is scaled by `gРђ▓` into u, then by `fРђ▓` into y, so the
rates multiply (exactly how backprop chains them). Plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 77 Рєњ 78.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**78 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed `dy/dx = (2 sin x)(cos x) = sin 2x`;

embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v297` Рєњ `atlas-v298`.

- **ps-uniform-exponential** Рђћ **the exponential is memoryless**: `P(X>5|X>3) = eРЂ╗┬╣ РЅѕ 0.368`, identical to a fresh `P(X>2)`;
  constant hazard rate, the only continuous distribution with this property.
- **c-antiderivatives** Рђћ **the power rule's n=Рѕњ1 exception**: `x^(n+1)/(n+1)` hits `├и0` at `n=Рѕњ1`, filled by `РѕФ(1/x)dx = ln|x|+C`
  Рђћ why `ln` appears throughout calculus.
- **dl-activation-functions** Рђћ **why sigmoid saturates**: `¤Ѓ'(0)=0.25` vs `¤Ѓ'(5)РЅѕ0.0066` (38├Ќ smaller); multiplied through depth
  Рєњ the vanishing gradient, which ReLU (gradient 1) dodges.

## iter 356 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on foundational lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across algo/calc/RL/LA (deep-dives 113 Рєњ **117**):
- **a-arrays-lists-stacks-queues** Рђћ **array vs linked list = a memory-layout tradeoff**: contiguous gives `O(1)` access +
  cache speed but `O(n)` mid-edits; linked gives `O(1)` edits but `O(n)` access + pointer-chasing.
- **c-optimization** Рђћ **РѕЄf=0 is necessary, not sufficient**: it flags minima, maxima, *and* saddles; the second-order
  condition + boundary checks (or convexity) finish the job.
- **rl-imitation** Рђћ **why behavioural cloning drifts**: per-step error compounds (РЅѕ`T┬▓`) as the agent strays into states the
  expert never visited; DAgger + inverse RL restore the broken i.i.d. assumption.
- **la-matrix-calculus-backprop** Рђћ **backprop = chain rule in the cheap multiplication order**: a scalar loss makes
  right-to-left (reverse-mode) Jacobian-vector products vastly cheaper Рђћ all gradients at ~one forward pass.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX (`\lt`/`\gt` for the second-order signs); injected byte-stably with round-trip +
even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.

  `y' = x╦Б(ln x + 1)`, `РЅѕ 6.77` at `x=2` Рђћ the move for variable base-and-exponent.

  factor* (choosing among ~7.4 equally likely tokens); why exponentiate log-space loss to a linear "number of choices."
- **ps-errors-and-power** Рђћ **power = 1Рѕњ╬▓ and its four levers**: `╬▓=0.20` Рєњ power `0.80`; raise it via larger `n`, bigger effect,
  higher `╬▒`, or less noise Рђћ with the `╬▒`/`╬▓` tension that only more data resolves.

## iter 352 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across algo/DL/PS/calc (deep-dives 109 Рєњ **113**):
- **a-graph-representations-traversal** Рђћ **BFS vs DFS = queue vs stack**: a FIFO queue fans out level-by-level (shortest
  paths); a LIFO stack dives deep (topo sort, cycle detection, components) Рђћ one traversal, swap the frontier; both `O(V+E)`.
- **dl-learning-rate-schedules-and-tuning** Рђћ **why warmup then decay**: warmup avoids early blow-up on noisy random-init
  gradients (and unreliable Adam variance estimates); decay settles into the minimum Рђћ cautious Рєњ confident Рєњ gentle.
- **ps-bernoulli-binomial** Рђћ **where `C(n,k)p^k(1Рѕњp)^(nРѕњk)` comes from**: `p^k(1Рѕњp)^(nРѕњk)` is one ordering's probability,
  `C(n,k)` counts the orderings Рђћ the coefficient exists only because we count *how many* successes, not *which*.
- **c-linearization-lhopital** Рђћ **why L'H├┤pital works**: near a `0/0` point each function РЅѕ its tangent line, the `(xРѕњa)`
  factors cancel, leaving the ratio of slopes `f'(a)/g'(a)` Рђћ `0/0` just means "depends how fast each goes to zero."
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 1 / 0 / 12 / 14 spans Рђћ BFS/DFS & warmup are
intentionally prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change.

- **c-implicit-related-rates** Рђћ **the expanding balloon**: `dV/dt = 4¤ђr┬▓ dr/dt` Рєњ at `r=5`, `dr/dt = 100/(100¤ђ) = 1/¤ђ РЅѕ 0.318`
  cm/s; constant inflation means the radius grows *slower* as the balloon enlarges (`dr/dt РѕЮ 1/r┬▓`).

  one-line `E[X] = 1 + (1Рѕњp)E[X]` derivation.
- **rl-value-iteration** Рђћ **value iteration as a contraction**: on a self-loop `V = 1 + 0.9V`, sweeps `0Рєњ1Рєњ1.9Рєњ2.71 Рєњ 10`,
  the error shrinking `├Ќ╬│` each pass (`10,9,8.1,7.29`) Рђћ Banach guarantees the unique fixed point `V*=10`.

## iter 347 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across RL/algo/LLM/calc (deep-dives 105 Рєњ **109**):
- **rl-eligibility-traces** Рђћ **TD(╬╗) dials between TD and Monte Carlo**: a fading trace decaying by `╬│╬╗` spreads credit
  backward; `╬╗=0` is TD(0), `╬╗=1` is MC Рђћ a bias-variance knob built into credit assignment.
- **a-comparison-sorts** Рђћ **quicksort vs mergesort, same ╬ў(n log n)**: quicksort in-place + cache-friendly (but `O(n┬▓)`
  worst case); mergesort stable + guaranteed `O(n log n)` (but `O(n)` space) Рђћ the tie breaks on constants/memory/stability.
- **l-safety-and-frontier** Рђћ **alignment is a proxy problem**: we optimize a reward-model proxy, not true intent; Goodhart Рєњ
  reward hacking, sycophancy, fluent falsehoods Рђћ no malice needed, just a misspecified objective.
- **c-extrema-curve-sketching** Рђћ **f' and f'' draw the whole shape**: `f'` sign gives rise/fall + critical points, `f''` sign
  gives concavity + inflection points Рђћ calculus as a recipe for sketching curves.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX (`\lt`/`\gt` for the derivative-sign inequalities); injected byte-stably with round-trip +
even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 5 / 5 / 0 / 13 spans Рђћ the proxy one is
