## iter 345 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across LA/calc/RL (examples 331 Рєњ **334**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **la-four-subspaces-rank** Рђћ **the four subspaces fit together by the numbers**: a `3├Ќ5` rank-2 matrix Рєњ col/row dim `2`,
  null dim `3`, left-null dim `1`; row+null `= 5 = n`, col+left-null `= 3 = m` (orthogonal complements filling each space).
- **c-differentiation-rules** Рђћ **the product rule** `(uv)' = u'v + uv'` (not `u'v'`): `x┬▓sin x Рєњ 2x sin x + x┬▓ cos x`, with the
  rectangle-area intuition for why the two strips add.
- **rl-exploration** Рђћ **UCB: optimism under uncertainty**: `x╠ё + cРѕџ(ln t / nРѓљ)` scores arm 2 (`1.615`) over arm 1 (`1.099`)
  despite a lower mean, because its bonus from few pulls is larger Рђћ regret grows only like `ln t`.

## iter 343 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on foundational lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across DL/algo/PS/calc (deep-dives 101 Рєњ **105**):
- **dl-the-artificial-neuron-and-mlp** Рђћ **one neuron is logistic regression; many are a universal approximator**: each unit is
  weighted-sum-then-squash; composition (not a new kind of arithmetic) is what makes a hidden layer approximate any function.
- **a-asymptotic-analysis** Рђћ **why Big-O throws away constants**: the fastest-growing term dominates for large `n`, so
  `5n┬▓+100n+9000 = O(n┬▓)`; an `O(n log n)` sort eventually beats `O(n┬▓)` whatever the constants.
- **ps-expectation-variance** Рђћ **expectation always adds; variance is pickier**: `E[X+Y]=E[X]+E[Y]` always, but
  `Var(X+Y)` needs `Cov=0` and `Var(aX+b)=a┬▓Var(X)` Рђћ the asymmetry behind the `1/n` shrinkage of an average's variance.
- **c-definite-integral-riemann** Рђћ **the integral is a limit of rectangles**: `РѕФ = lim ╬Б f(xрхб)╬ћx`; the `РѕФ` is a stretched "S"
  for sum Рђћ addition taken to a limit, which is why the Fundamental Theorem is so surprising.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 3 / 18 / 9 / 13 spans) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;
all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v282` Рєњ `atlas-v283`.

## iter 341 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across algo/calc/LA (examples 328 Рєњ **331**, all РїўK-searchable):
- **a-trees-heaps** Рђћ **a heap needs no pointers**: 0-indexed array, node `i` Рєњ parent `Рїі(iРѕњ1)/2РїІ`, children `2i+1`, `2i+2`;
  for `i=3` that's parent `1`, children `7, 8` Рђћ a complete tree maps to memory as pure arithmetic.
- **c-computing-limits** Рђћ **limits at infinity**: divide by the highest power Рєњ `(3x┬▓+2x)/(x┬▓Рѕњ5) Рєњ 3` (ratio of leading
  coefficients for equal degrees); lower-degree top Рєњ `0`, higher Рєњ `┬▒Рѕъ`.
- **la-symmetric-spectral** Рђћ **spectral decomposition as a sum of rank-1 projections**: `A = ╬Б╬╗рхбqрхбqрхбрхђ`; for `[[2,1],[1,2]]`,
  `3┬и┬й[[1,1],[1,1]] + 1┬и┬й[[1,Рѕњ1],[Рѕњ1,1]]` reconstructs `A` Рђћ the engine behind PCA and matrix functions.
Every value node-verified (parent 1/children 7,8; limit 3; matrix reconstructs); injected byte-stably with round-trip +

## iter 339 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives Рђћ past 100 (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across DL/RL/algo/PS take the codex past a milestone (deep-dives 97 Рєњ **101**):
- **dl-activation-functions** Рђћ **without nonlinearity, depth is an illusion**: stacked linear layers collapse to one affine
  map (`WРѓѓWРѓЂ`); the activation between them is the entire reason depth buys expressiveness.
- **rl-value-approximation** Рђћ **the deadly triad**: function approximation + bootstrapping + off-policy together can make the
  update a non-contraction and diverge; DQN's target network + replay buffer defuse exactly those three.
- **a-binary-search** Рђћ **the invariant and the bugs that ignore it**: target stays in `[lo,hi]`; `lo=mid` (not `mid+1`) loops
  forever, and `(lo+hi)/2` overflows Рђћ `lo+(hiРѕњlo)/2` doesn't.
- **ps-normal-distribution** Рђћ **why the normal is everywhere**: the CLT (additive randomness), maximum entropy (least-assuming
  for a given mean/variance), and convenience (closed under sums, the 68РђЊ95РђЊ99.7 rule).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 6 / 0 / 9 / 10 spans Рђћ the deadly-triad one is
intentionally prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change.

## iter 337 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across DL/calc/RL (examples 325 Рєњ **328**, all now РїўK-searchable):
- **dl-convolution-operation** Рђћ **two 3├Ќ3 convs beat one 5├Ќ5**: same `5├Ќ5` receptive field, but `2├Ќ9=18` weights vs `25`
  (and a ReLU between them adds nonlinearity) Рђћ the VGG insight behind `3├Ќ3` as the default.
- **c-derivative-definition** Рђћ **a corner where the derivative fails**: `|x|` at `0` has right-limit `+1`, left-limit `Рѕњ1`;
  the two-sided limit disagrees, so `f'(0)` doesn't exist Рђћ continuity isn't enough for differentiability.
- **rl-td-learning** Рђћ **TD vs Monte Carlo targets** on one step: MC aims at the actual return `G=3` (unbiased, high-variance);
  TD bootstraps `r+╬│V(s')=5.5` (lower-variance, biased) Рђћ same update form, different target.
Every value node-verified (18<25; ┬▒1 corner; 5.5 vs 3); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +

## iter 335 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across DL/DL/LLM/algo (deep-dives 93 Рєњ **97**):
- **dl-convolution-operation** Рђћ **weight-sharing + locality**: a conv layer is a restricted matmul (`3├Ќ3` = 9 weights, not
  millions); the local+shared restriction is the inductive bias that makes it translation-equivariant and data-efficient.
- **dl-practical-training-and-debugging** Рђћ **the order to debug**: overfit a tiny batch first (isolates code bugs), check the
  initial loss (`РЅѕ ln C`), *then* tune the learning rate Рђћ top-down (code Рєњ data Рєњ optimization Рєњ capacity).
- **l-decoding-strategies** Рђћ **temperature/top-k/top-p as three knobs on one trade-off**: `T` reshapes the whole distribution,
  top-k/top-p truncate its tail; they compose along the coherence-vs-diversity dial.
- **a-shortest-paths-topo** Рђћ **why Dijkstra needs non-negative edges**: greedy settling is valid only if no detour can
  shorten a settled distance; negatives break it Рєњ Bellman-Ford relaxes `VРѕњ1├Ќ` at `O(VE)` (DAGs: one topo pass, `O(V+E)`).
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX (`\lt`/`\gt` for the temperature inequalities); injected byte-stably with round-trip +
even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + unsupported-env guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 2 / 2 / 9 / 4 spans) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;
all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v274` Рєњ `atlas-v275`.

at b=2 (where the curves meet). The note drives home the one rule: subtract the lower curve from the upper, then integrate.

## iter 332 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across calc/algo/RL (examples 322 Рєњ **325**):
- **c-optimization** Рђћ **max area, fixed perimeter**: `20 m` of fence Рєњ `A(x)=x(10Рѕњx)`, `A'=0 РЄњ x=5`, a `5├Ќ5` square with
  area `25 m┬▓` Рђћ constraint-optimization pushes toward symmetry (existing: minimize a quadratic + a GD step).
- **a-comparison-sorts** Рђћ **the ╬Е(n log n) lower bound from counting leaves**: a sort's decision tree needs `n!` leaves, so
  height `РЅЦ logРѓѓ(n!)`; for `n=4`, `logРѓѓ24 РЅѕ 4.58` Рєњ **РЅЦ5** comparisons; Stirling gives `╬ў(n log n)` (existing: 3-element
  decision tree + merge-vs-insertion).
- **rl-mdp-formalism** Рђћ **why discounting bounds the return**: `G=╬Б╬│рхЌ┬и1 = 1/(1Рѕњ╬│)`, so `╬│=0.9 Рєњ 10` (finite though rewards
  never stop); `|G| РЅц r_max/(1Рѕњ╬│)` makes value functions well-defined (existing: model an MDP + compute a return).
Every value node-verified (25; logРѓѓ24РЅѕ4.58Рєњ5; 10 & 2); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance +

## iter 331 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across LLM/algo/PS/LA (deep-dives 89 Рєњ **93**):
- **l-transformer-block** Рђћ **mix-then-think, wrapped in residuals**: attention communicates across tokens, the FFN computes
  per-token; residual `x + Sublayer(x)` + layer-norm make depth trainable (РЅѕРЁћ of params live in the FFNs).
- **a-union-find-range** Рђћ **near-O(1) by being lazy and flat**: union-by-rank keeps trees shallow, path compression flattens
  them on every `find`; together `O(╬▒(n))`, effectively constant.
- **ps-covariance-correlation** Рђћ **correlation = covariance with units divided out**: `¤Ђ = Cov/(¤ЃРѓЊ¤Ѓ_y) Рѕѕ [Рѕњ1,1]`; covariance
  gives direction, correlation strength Рђћ both see only *linear* structure (`¤Ђ=0` РЅа independent).
- **la-matrix-multiplication** Рђћ **it's function composition**: `AB` = "do B then A", forced by `(AB)x=A(Bx)`; hence
  non-commutativity, the shape-matching rule, and associativity all fall out Рђћ no rule to memorize.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance + the new
unsupported-env guard.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 1 / 4 / 7 / 23 spans) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**;
all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v270` Рєњ `atlas-v271`.

## iter 330 Рђћ Step-back: full kErr + route sweep (clean) + gate hardened vs mathtools envs (workflow / broken-proofing)
**Round-number step-back (iter 330),** triggered by the iter-329 discovery that a `psmallmatrix` KaTeX error had shipped
unnoticed (static lints miss it; only `--dump-dom` kErr catches it). So this step-back's sweep was a **kErr audit**:
- **Full kErr sweep Рђћ all 148 lessons**, revealing *every* example and opening *every* deep-dive Рєњ **errs=0, kErr=0, 0 bad
  lessons**. The psmallmatrix was the *only* KaTeX error in the entire codex; all math now renders clean.
- **All non-lesson routes** (courses, Lab + all 71 widgets, review/test/map/glossary/achievements/stats/playground/library/
  notes) Рєњ **errs=0, kErr=0, bad=none**. Total **237 routes** green.
**Reflection (321РђЊ329):** content(dd) ┬и UI/UX ┬и code ┬и viz ┬и examples ┬и content(dd) ┬и code ┬и viz ┬и examples+fix Рђћ healthy
rotation across every modality plus two "amplify" UI/UX moves; all verified and pushed; one real bug found and fixed. Codex
is mature and exhaustively complete (148 lessons ┬и 71 viz ┬и 89 deep-dives ┬и 322 examples ┬и 45 code lessons ┬и 138 glossary).
**The ship (broken-proofing the loop).** Hardened `gate.js` with an **`UNSUPPORTED_KATEX_ENV` lint** so the exact class of
bug can't recur: it flags the `[pbBvV]smallmatrix` family and starred `matrix*/cases*` variants (mathtools envs this KaTeX
build can't parse), which previously raised a real `.katex-error` invisible to the static $-parity/tag lints. Now caught at
the cheap `node gate.js` step, no Chrome needed.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (zero false positives across the corpus Рђћ it only uses supported envs); **self-test** Рђћ reintroducing
a `psmallmatrix` makes the gate FAIL with the new message, and removing it returns to green; `gate.js` is dev-only (not in
`sw.js`), so no cache bump. The two runtime sweeps above confirm the live site is clean. No save-shape change, no asset change.

## iter 329 Рђћ Three more worked examples + a KaTeX render-bug fix (examples / broken)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons across LA/algo/calc (examples 319 Рєњ **322**):
- **la-inverse-and-systems** Рђћ **when there's no unique solution**: a singular `A=[[1,1],[2,2]]` (`det=0`); `x+y=2, 2x+2y=5`
  has **no** solution (parallel distinct lines), `x+y=2, 2x+2y=4` has **infinitely many** (same line) Рђћ the det=0 dichotomy.
- **a-graph-representations-traversal** Рђћ **cycle detection with DFS**: a gray (on-stack) vertex reached by an edge is a
  **back edge** Рєњ cycle (`AРєњBРєњCРєњA`); a DAG has none, so topological sort succeeds.
- **c-convexity** Рђћ **Jensen's inequality**: convex `f`, `f(E[X]) РЅц E[f(X)]`; with `x┬▓` on `{1,3}`, `f(2)=4 РЅц 5=E[f(X)]`,
  and the gap `1` is exactly `Var(X)` Рђћ convexity turns spread into upward bias.
**Bonus fix (broken render).** While verifying, found a **pre-existing KaTeX error** in the existing MSE-convexity example:
`\begin{psmallmatrix}` needs the mathtools package, which this KaTeX build lacks (`No such environment: psmallmatrix`).
Replaced both with `\left(\begin{smallmatrix}Рђд\end{smallmatrix}\right)` (supported, keeps the parentheses) Рђћ c-convexity
now renders **kErr=0**.
Every value node-verified; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**322 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** each Examples tab shows 3, reveals with KaTeX
(62 / 137 / 41 spans) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0** (the psmallmatrix error gone); all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**.
No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v269` Рєњ `atlas-v270`.

## iter 328 Рђћ Newton's-method visualizer Рђћ 71st widget (visualizations)
No widget showed Newton's method, a flagship numerical root-finder Рђћ and it's *iterated linearization*, so it fits the
linear-approximation lesson perfectly. Added the **71st Lab widget `calc-newton`** (a 2nd viz on `c-linearization-lhopital`):
on `f(x)=x┬▓Рѕњ2`, **Step** draws the tangent at the current guess down to where it crosses the x-axis Рђћ that crossing is the
next guess `x Рєљ x Рѕњ f(x)/fРђ▓(x)` Рђћ and **Run** iterates to the root. The guesses `2 Рєњ 1.5 Рєњ 1.4167 Рєњ 1.41421` rocket toward
**Рѕџ2** (gold marker), with the note explaining the **quadratic** convergence (correct digits roughly double per step).
Plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 70 Рєњ 71.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**71 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed the iterates converge to Рѕџ2; **via
`--dump-dom`** Step gives `x = 1.500000` and Run reaches `1.414214` (within 1e-4 of Рѕџ2), `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson
embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v268` Рєњ `atlas-v269`.

  of sums needed.

  post-norm `mean var` is `0.00 1.00`.
- **a-hash-tables** Рђћ **hashing** `h(k)=k mod m`: keys `[10,22,31,4,15]` mod 7 Рєњ buckets `3,1,3,4,1` (10 & 31 collide, 22 & 15 collide).

## iter 326 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
4 "Deeper dive" expandables across DL/RL/LA/calc (deep-dives 85 Рєњ **89**):
- **dl-embeddings-and-tokenization** Рђћ embeddings turn **symbols into geometry**: a *trainable* lookup table where proximity
  = learned similarity ("king Рѕњ man + woman РЅѕ queen"); meaning stored as positions, not definitions.
- **rl-policy-iteration** Рђћ a **monotone two-step dance**: evaluate `Vрхќ`, then act greedily w.r.t. it; the policy-improvement
  theorem makes every step uphill, so it reaches optimal in finitely many iterations.
- **la-gradients-jacobians** Рђћ the **Jacobian is the derivative generalized**: an `m├Ќn` matrix of all partials, best linear
  approx `f(x+╬┤)РЅѕf(x)+J╬┤`; the gradient is its `m=1` case, layout = a transpose-consistency choice for backprop.
- **c-partial-derivatives** Рђћ a partial **freezes every axis but one**; assembled, the partials give the gradient, the tangent
  plane, and (Clairaut) commuting mixed partials Рђћ multivariable calculus = one-variable calculus per axis.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 0 / 3 / 7 / 7 spans Рђћ embeddings is intentionally
prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v266` Рєњ `atlas-v267`.

## iter 325 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons, each a new technique across algo/PS/calc (examples 316 Рєњ **319**):
- **a-divide-and-conquer** Рђћ **Karatsuba**: multiply `12 ├Ќ 34` with **three** products not four (`ac=3, bd=8, (a+b)(c+d)=21`
  Рєњ cross term `10` by subtraction) Рєњ `408`; recursively this is `O(n^1.585)` (existing: merge-sort trace + a recurrence).
- **ps-bernoulli-binomial** Рђћ **mean & variance of a binomial** as a sum of Bernoullis: `np = 6`, `np(1Рѕњp) = 4.2`,
  `sd РЅѕ 2.05`; why `p(1Рѕњp)` peaks at `p=0.5` (existing: heads-in-5-flips PMF + defective bulbs).
- **c-extrema-curve-sketching** Рђћ **closed-interval extreme values**: `x┬│Рѕњ3x` on `[0,2]` Рєњ check the critical point `x=1`
  *and* the endpoints; the max `2` is at the **endpoint** `x=2`, which the derivative test alone would miss (existing:
  first- and second-derivative classification).
Every value node-verified (408; mean 6 / var 4.2; max 2 / min Рѕњ2); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` +

## iter 324 Рђћ Geometric-distribution visualizer Рђћ 70th widget (visualizations)
`ps-geometric-waiting` had no widget. Added the **70th Lab widget `ps-geometric`**, embedded there: a bar chart of the
geometric PMF `P(X=k) = (1Рѕњp)^(kРѕњ1)┬иp` Рђћ the probability the first success lands on trial `k`. Slide the **success
probability p** (0.1РђЊ0.9): the bars decay geometrically (each is `(1Рѕњp)├Ќ` the previous), and a dashed line marks the
**mean 1/p**. The note explains it's **memoryless** Рђћ past failures don't bring success closer, the discrete twin of the
exponential. Plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 69 Рєњ 70.

## iter 321 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Back to the owner's depth directive Рђћ 4 "Deeper dive" expandables across LLM/algo/PS/calc (deep-dives 81 Рєњ **85**):
- **l-finetuning-and-instruction-tuning** Рђћ SFT shapes **behaviour, not knowledge**: the objective stays next-token
  prediction; only the *data* changes (helpful-assistant demonstrations), so "follow the instruction" becomes the likely
  continuation.
- **a-correctness-invariants** Рђћ a **loop invariant is induction**: initialization (base case), maintenance (inductive step),
  termination РЪ╣ correctness Рђћ testing shows a bug's presence, an invariant shows its absence.
- **ps-random-variables-distributions** Рђћ a **random variable is a function** $X$ from outcomes to numbers; its distribution
  is the pushforward of the sample-space probabilities onto the number line Рђћ what makes probability quantitative.
- **c-antiderivatives** Рђћ why the **+C**: differentiation kills constants ($\frac{d}{dx}C=0$), so antidifferentiation recovers
  a whole *family* of curves, pinned down only by an initial condition Рђћ and why integration is harder than differentiation.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards (incl. `<code>`).

## iter 319 Рђћ Joint-distribution visualizer Рђћ 69th widget (visualizations)
`ps-joint-distributions` had no widget, and the joint Рєњ marginals Рєњ independence chain is core probability. Added the
**69th Lab widget `ps-joint`**, embedded there: a 5├Ќ5 **P(X,Y) heatmap** (cell shade РѕЮ probability, value shown) with
**marginal bars** Рђћ `P(X)` summed down each column (below), `P(Y)` summed across each row (left). Two presets: **Independent**
(the joint is exactly `P(X)┬иP(Y)`, an outer product Рєњ independence error **0**) and **Correlated** (mass clusters on the
diagonal Рєњ the joint no longer factors, error **0.083**). The note explains marginals = summing the joint, and independence
РЪ║ joint = product of marginals. Plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 68 Рєњ 69.

## iter 318 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons, each a new technique across DL/PS/algorithms (examples 313 Рєњ **316**):
- **dl-attention-mechanism** Рђћ **why divide by РѕџdРѓќ**: unscaled scores `[16,20]` Рєњ `softmax РЅѕ [0.018, 0.982]` (saturated,
  dead gradient); scaled by 8 Рєњ `[2,2.5]` Рєњ `[0.378, 0.622]` (healthy) Рђћ dot products grow like `РѕџdРѓќ`, so the divide holds
  the scale steady (existing: single-query attention + causal self-attention).
- **ps-poisson** Рђћ **P(at least one)** via the complement: `1 Рѕњ e^(Рѕњ╬╗)` with `╬╗=3` Рєњ `1 Рѕњ 0.0498 РЅѕ 0.950` (existing:
  defects + binomial-to-Poisson).
- **a-hash-tables** Рђћ **load factor & resizing**: `╬▒ = 6/8 = 0.75`, resize-and-rehash to `m=16` Рєњ `╬▒ = 0.375`, with the
  amortized-`O(1)` accounting behind the rare `O(n)` resize (existing: chaining insert + linear-probing deletion).
Every value node-verified; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + `<strong>`/`<em>`/`<b>` tag-balance pre-guards.

## iter 317 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Steering to the topics the last few dd batches skipped (RL/calc/LA/DL) Рђћ 4 "Deeper dive" expandables (deep-dives 77 Рєњ **81**):
- **rl-offline** Рђћ offline RL fights **distribution shift**: value estimates extrapolate wildly on actions the dataset never
  tried, so the fix is conservatism (CQL/BCQ) Рђћ don't trust values where you have no data.
- **la-projection-least-squares** Рђћ least squares **is** an orthogonal projection: `Ax╠ѓ` is `b`'s shadow on the column space,
  the residual РіЦ the columns gives `AрхђAx╠ѓ = Aрхђb` Рђћ dropping a perpendicular, not curve-fitting magic.
- **c-continuity** Рђћ the **IVT** guarantees a root exists (sign change Рєњ `f(c)=0` somewhere) before you find it Рђћ the basis
  of bisection.
- **dl-transfer-learning** Рђћ why pretrained features transfer: early layers learn **general** representations (edges,
  syntax), so reuse the backbone + retrain the head Рђћ amortizing the expensive part once.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards (used `\lt`/`\gt`
for the IVT sign inequalities to dodge the `<`-in-math landmine).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 0 / 10 / 10 / 0 spans Рђћ offline-RL & transfer
are intentionally prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change.
SW cache `atlas-v257` Рєњ `atlas-v258`.

## iter 316 Рђћ Kruskal's MST visualizer Рђћ 68th widget (visualizations)
`a-mst-union-find` had no widget, and Kruskal's algorithm is a perfect visual: greedy + a union-find cycle test. Added the
**68th Lab widget `algo-kruskal`**, embedded there: a 6-node weighted graph where **Step** considers edges cheapest-first Рђћ
adding one in **sage** if its ends are in different components, or **rejecting** it in dashed **rust** when union-find finds
they're already connected (a cycle). **Run** completes it, **Reset** restarts. The note narrates each decision and the
running tree weight, which climbs to the minimum **13** (5 edges). Plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete`
fallback 67 Рєњ 68.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**68 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed the MST (edges 1-2,1-3,3-4,0-2,3-5;
weight 13; 4 cycle-edges rejected); **via `--dump-dom`** Step adds the lightest edge (1РђЊ2, w=1), **Run Рєњ weight 13, 5 edges,
complete**, `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape
change. SW cache `atlas-v256` Рєњ `atlas-v257`.
(Tooling landmine: `node -e 'Рђд'` inline breaks on apostrophes Рђћ e.g. "Kruskal's" closes the quote and zsh glob-errors on the
rest; use a heredoc script file for README/doc edits with apostrophes. Re-did the README update that way.)

## iter 315 Рђћ Three more code exercises across the 4-count topics (new functionality / active learning)
With code exercises evenly spread (most topics at 4), added **3 more gate-verified JavaScript exercises** across LA/RL/PS

  Рєњ `3 1`.
- **rl-td-learning** Рђћ one **TD(0) update** `V(s) Рєљ V(s) + ╬▒[r + ╬│V(sРђ▓) Рѕњ V(s)]`: V=5, r=1, ╬│=0.9, VРђ▓=10, ╬▒=0.1 Рєњ `5.50`
  (TD error +5, scaled by ╬▒).
- **ps-point-estimation** Рђћ **sample standard deviation** dividing by `nРѕњ1` (Bessel): `[2,4,6,8]` Рєњ `2.58` Рђћ ties to that
  lesson's deeper-dive on why it's `nРѕњ1`.

## iter 314 Рђћ In-app "reduce motion" toggle (accessibility)
Every animation already respected the OS `prefers-reduced-motion` setting, but a user who wants calmer motion had **no in-app
control** Рђћ they'd have to change a system-wide OS preference. Added a **"Reduce motion" toggle in Settings** (a11y best
practice: offer motion control in the app itself). It sets `data-reduce-motion="on"` on `<html>`, which (a) makes the shared
`reducedMotion()` helper return true Рђћ so every JS-driven animation already gated on it (confetti, sweep-bars, cascades,
count-ups, the map reveal, card flips, fly-outs) is suppressed Рђћ and (b) triggers a **CSS guard mirroring the OS-pref block**
(`[data-reduce-motion="on"] *` zeroes animation/transition durations; `.reveal` shows instantly). Persisted to localStorage
and re-applied on boot; the button is `aria-pressed` and relabels on toggle. Independent of (and additive to) the OS setting.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** the toggle is off by default (`data-reduce-motion=null`), turning it on sets
the attribute + persists (`localStorage.reduceMotion=1`, `aria-pressed=true`, label "­Ъї┐ Reduced motion: on"); with the pref
**preset, boot re-applies it** (`data-reduce-motion=on`); all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape
change. SW cache `atlas-v254` Рєњ `atlas-v255`.

## iter 313 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Continuing the depth directive Рђћ 4 "Deeper dive" expandables across LLM/DL/algo/PS (deep-dives 73 Рєњ **77**):
- **l-optimization-and-stability** Рђћ why LLM training needs **warmup** (early gradients are huge/noisy), **AdamW's decoupled
  decay** (decay shouldn't be scaled by the adaptive step), and **gradient clipping** (one bad batch can't blow up) Рђћ it's
  about *stability*, not just speed.
- **dl-pooling-and-cnn-architectures** Рђћ pooling buys **translation invariance** (a feature anywhere in the window survives)
  *and* a **growing receptive field**: convolution detects *what*, pooling forgets *exactly where*.
- **a-approximation-randomized** Рђћ when "exact and fast" is impossible for NP-hard problems, keep a **guarantee**:
  provably-near-optimal (a `c`-approximation) or probably-correct (randomized).
- **ps-sample-spaces-events** Рђћ probability is **set theory with a measure**: sample space `╬Е`, events as subsets, three
  axioms; "or/and/not" are union/intersection/complement, and inclusion-exclusion is just no-double-counting.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 0 / 0 / 2 / 7 spans Рђћ the LLM-opt & pooling
dives are intentionally prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape
change. SW cache `atlas-v253` Рєњ `atlas-v254`.

## iter 312 Рђћ Conditional-expectation visualizer Рђћ 67th widget (visualizations)
`ps-conditional-expectation` had a deeper-dive but no widget, and E[Y|X] is the bridge from probability to ML (regression
*is* conditional expectation). Added the **67th Lab widget `ps-conditional-expectation`**, embedded there: a scatter of Y
against X (here `y = 3 + 2sin(x)` + noise), with X sliced into 12 bins Рђћ the **average Y per slice** is plotted as the
**E[Y|X] curve** (violet), threading the noisy cloud and tracing the true relationship (dashed, toggleable). A **noise
slider** shows the slice-averages still recover the curve as noise grows (averaging cancels it). The note's takeaway:
regression learns exactly this curve, and a straight-line fit is just its best linear approximation. Deterministic
reproducible jitter (no `Math.random`), plain-unicode note (no `$`). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 66 Рєњ 67.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**67 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed binned means track `f(x)` (max
`|meanРѕњf|` РЅѕ 0.26, noise averaging out); **via `--dump-dom`** the viz renders, the note shows `E[Y|X]`, the noise slider and
true-curve toggle both update, `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0
(12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v252` Рєњ `atlas-v253`.

## iter 311 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons, each a new technique across LA/PS/calc (examples 310 Рєњ **313**):
- **la-svd** Рђћ **best rank-1 approximation** (Eckart-Young): with singular values 5 and 3, the rank-1 truncation keeps
  `25/34 РЅѕ 73.5%` of the energy and drops only `¤ЃРѓѓ` Рђћ the basis of SVD/PCA compression (existing: a full 2├Ќ2 SVD + a
  rank-deficient one).
- **ps-t-tests** Рђћ the **paired t-test** as a one-sample test on the differences: `d=[2,4,3]` Рєњ `d╠ё=3, s=1, tРЅѕ5.20` on 2 df,
  and why pairing cancels between-subject variation for more power (existing: one-sample + two-sample).
- **c-area-volume** Рђћ **volume by cylindrical shells**: `y=x┬▓` on `[0,2]` about the y-axis Рєњ `РѕФ2¤ђx┬иx┬▓ dx = 8¤ђ РЅѕ 25.13`,
  and when shells beat washers (no inversion needed) (existing: area-between-curves + a washer volume).
Every value node-verified (73.5%; t=5.196; 8¤ђ); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + tag-balance pre-guards.

## iter 307 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Continuing the depth directive, deliberately steering to the **less-recently-covered topics** (PS/RL/algo/calc) Рђћ 4 "Deeper
dive" expandables (deep-dives 69 Рєњ **73**):
- **ps-uniform-exponential** Рђћ the exponential **forgets**: `P(X>s+t | X>s)=P(X>t)` because `e^{-╬╗(s+t)}/e^{-╬╗s}=e^{-╬╗t}` Рђћ
  the only memoryless continuous law (no aging, no internal clock).
- **rl-model-based** Рђћ a model lets you **plan**: learn `P(s'|s,a)` and `R(s,a)`, then simulate rollouts / tree-search
  (AlphaZero) for sample efficiency Рђћ at the cost of compounding model error.
- **a-divide-and-conquer** Рђћ why **balanced splits** win: halving makes the recursion tree `logРѓѓn` deep, so linear combines
  total `O(n log n)`; uneven splits collapse to `O(n┬▓)` Рђћ the win is geometric shrinkage, decided by the master theorem.
- **c-integration-techniques** Рђћ the tricks are **derivative rules reversed**: substitution is the chain rule backward,
  integration by parts is the product rule backward Рђћ "what was this the derivative of?"
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 6 / 2 / 6 / 7 spans) with **kErr=0,
rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v247` Рєњ `atlas-v248`.

## iter 306 Рђћ Binary Min-Heap visualizer Рђћ 66th widget (visualizations)
`a-trees-heaps` had no widget, and the heap is one of the most important data structures Рђћ its arrayРєћtree duality is a
classic "click" moment. Added the **66th Lab widget `algo-heap`**, embedded there: the same values drawn **as a binary tree
above and the contiguous array below**, with the **minimum at the root** (index 0). **+ Insert** pushes a random value and
**sifts it up** (the swap path lights gold); **Extract min** removes the root, moves the last leaf up, and **sifts it down**;
**Reset** restores. The note carries the live array (`[3, 7, 5, Рђд]`) Рђћ verifiable *and* screen-reader-readable, since the
canvas array row isn't. Confirmed the gamification achievement set is already exhaustive (58 across every behavior), so this
viz is the higher-value ship.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**66 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed siftUp/siftDown keep the min-heap
property; **via `--dump-dom`** after 3 inserts (9 elts) and an extract (8 elts) the array parsed from the note is a **valid
min-heap with root = min** both times, `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0
(12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v246` Рєњ `atlas-v247`.

## iter 305 Рђћ The Knowledge Map draws its constellation on landing (animation)
The flagship Knowledge Map rendered its ~148-node SVG constellation **instantly** Рђћ no reveal. Added a **one-time staged
"draw-in"** when you land on the map: the colored **sector wedges + rings** fade first, then the **prerequisite edges**, then
the **nodes** pop in last (a structure Рєњ connections Рєњ concepts reveal). Pure CSS opacity animation (the nodes carry an SVG
`transform`, so opacity-only avoids clobbering their positions). The `drawing` class is added **before first paint** (set
synchronously right after `innerHTML`, so no flash) and **removed after ~1s** so the hover/keyboard-dim opacity control
resumes cleanly. Reduced-motion safe (the global guard zeroes animation durations; the JS also skips it under
`prefers-reduced-motion`). Confirmed it never strands the map invisible: even if the animation didn't run, fill-mode and the
1s class-removal leave every node at the default opacity 1.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** the map adds `drawing` on load and **removes it after 1s** (`drawingEarly=true`,
`drawingLate=false`), all **148 nodes** render and end at **opacity 1**, `errs=0`; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0
(12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v245` Рєњ `atlas-v246`.

## iter 304 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
A **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons at two, each a new technique across LA/algo/RL (examples 307 Рєњ **310**):
- **la-matrix-multiplication** Рђћ `Ax` two ways: the row view (dot products Рєњ `[4,10]`) *and* the column view (`2┬и[1,3]+1┬и[2,4]`),
  the picture that explains column space, span, and when `Ax=b` is solvable (existing: composing maps + non-square products).
- **a-binary-search** Рђћ the **`lower_bound`** variant traced on `[1,3,3,5,7]` for `3`: the half-open window converges to the
  **first** index `РЅЦ` target (index 1), the deterministic workhorse behind insertion points, counts, and ranges (existing:
  a plain trace + the O(log n) argument).
- **rl-policies-values** Рђћ **V from Q**: `V_¤ђ(s)=╬Б ¤ђ(a|s)┬иQ(s,a)` Рђћ greedy `¤ђ=[1,0]` Рєњ 10, stochastic `[0.7,0.3]` Рєњ 8.2, so
  exploring lowers value and the `1.8` gap is the price of randomness (existing: policy eval on a chain + greedy selection).
Every value node-verified ([4,10]; lower_bound=1; V=8.2/gap=1.8); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` +
`<strong>`/`<em>`/`<code>` tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**310 examples**); **via `--dump-dom`** each Examples tab shows 3, the new solution reveals with
KaTeX (117 / 28 / 97 spans Рђћ matrices render) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**.
No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v244` Рєњ `atlas-v245`.

## iter 303 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Continuing the owner's depth directive with **4 "Deeper dive" expandables** across four topics (deep-dives 65 Рєњ **69**):
- **la-orthonormal-gram-schmidt** Рђћ why orthonormal bases are computationally golden: `QрхђQ=I` makes the inverse free
  (`QРЂ╗┬╣=Qрхђ`), coordinates mere dot products, and maps length-preserving (numerically stable) Рђћ the payoff for Gram-Schmidt's work.
- **dl-overfitting-and-regularization** Рђћ regularization as a vote for simpler explanations: L2 (`╬╗РђќwРђќ┬▓`) makes big weights
  expensive, dropout spreads bets, early-stopping halts before memorization Рђћ all the same "prefer the simplest fit" prior.
- **l-rag-and-tools** Рђћ RAG separates *what a model knows* (a retrievable, updatable, citable store) from *how it reasons*
  (the frozen weights) Рђћ turning a closed-book exam into an open-book one.
- **c-gradient-descent-convergence** Рђћ why GD zigzags: the condition number `╬║=╬╗_max/╬╗_min` mismatches curvature across
  directions, so one learning rate can't serve all Рђћ the problem momentum/Adam/preconditioning fix.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each renders (KaTeX 6 / 2 / 0 / 6 spans Рђћ RAG is intentionally
prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache
`atlas-v243` Рєњ `atlas-v244`.

## iter 302 Рђћ The ╬хРђЊ╬┤ limit visualizer Рђћ 65th widget (visualizations)
`c-limits-intuition` had no widget, and the ╬хРђЊ╬┤ definition is the single most-feared idea in early calculus. Added the
**65th Lab widget `calc-limit-epsilon`**, embedded in that lesson: pick a tolerance **╬х** (a sage horizontal band around the
limit **L**) and the widget computes the **╬┤** interval around **a** (violet) that keeps the whole curve inside the band.
Shrink ╬х and ╬┤ shrinks with it Рђћ but a ╬┤ always exists, which *is* the definition (Рѕђ╬х РѕЃ╬┤). Two presets: the classic
`(x┬▓Рѕњ1)/(xРѕњ1)` (a line with a **removable hole** at a Рђћ an open circle, showing the limit ignores `f(a)` entirely, here
╬┤ = ╬х) and `x┬▓` (where ╬┤ < ╬х and is asymmetric). ╬┤ is found numerically by scanning outward from a until the curve leaves
the band. Plain-unicode note (no `$`, viz-note landmine). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 64 Рєњ 65.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**65 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed ╬┤ РЅѕ ╬х for the hole (0.60Рєњ0.60,
0.10Рєњ0.10) and ╬┤ < ╬х for x┬▓ (0.6Рєњ0.26, 0.1Рєњ0.05); **via `--dump-dom`** the ╬х slider drives ╬┤ (`0.60Рєњ0.10`), the function
toggle switches presets (x┬▓, ╬┤=0.05 at ╬х=0.10), `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke
**errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v242` Рєњ `atlas-v243`.

## iter 299 Рђћ Three more interactive code exercises across thin topics (new functionality / active learning)
Rebalancing the gate-verified code exercises (LA/calc/DL/RL/PS were all tied at the floor of 3): added **3 JavaScript
exercises** (lessons-with-code 30 Рєњ **33**; the gate now runs **28**), each a flagship numerical method:
- **la-inverse-and-systems** Рђћ **Cramer's rule** for a 2├Ќ2 system: `2x+y=5, x+3y=10` Рєњ each unknown is a determinant ratio
  Рєњ `1 3`.
- **rl-value-iteration** Рђћ one **Bellman optimality update** `V(s)=max_a[r+╬│┬иV(next)]`: the patient action `0+0.9┬и10` beats
  the greedy `1+0.9┬и5` Рєњ `9.0`.
- **c-intro-differential-equations** Рђћ **Euler's method** stepping the slope field for `y'=y`, `y(0)=1`, `h=0.5` Рєњ `2.25`
  (undershooting the true `eРЅѕ2.718`) Рђћ pairs with the slope-field deeper-dive from iter 289.
Every output node-verified; injected byte-stably with a round-trip guard + the injector refusing any lesson with existing
`data-code`.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**28 code-exercises verified** Рђћ all three outputs match `data-expected`); **via `--dump-dom`** the
rl-value-iteration widget hydrates, runs Рєњ `9.0`, "РюЊ Output matches expected"; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**.
No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v239` Рєњ `atlas-v240`.

## iter 298 Рђћ Accessible flashcard flip (accessibility)
A real a11y bug in the core study mode: both faces of the 3-D flashcard were always in the DOM with **no `aria-hidden`**,
so a screen reader read the **answer aloud before you flipped** Рђћ leaking the answer and defeating the whole point of
retrieval practice Рђћ while the card itself wasn't announced as interactive. Fixed:
- The card is now a proper **`role="button"` `tabindex="0"`** with an `aria-label` ("Flashcard Рђћ activate to reveal the
  answer", updated to "Answer revealed Рђћ grade how well you recalled it" on flip). Enter/Space already flipped it via the
  global handler; now SR users are told it's interactive and can focus it.
- The hidden face is kept **out of the accessibility tree**: `aria-hidden` toggles by flip state (back hidden until you
  flip, front hidden after), so SR users get the prompt first and the answer only on reveal Рђћ same as sighted users.
- The card's inner is an **`aria-live="polite"`** region, so flipping announces the revealed answer.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** the card renders as `role=button tabindex=0` with `aria-live=polite`; before
flip front is visible / back `aria-hidden=true`, after a click front `aria-hidden=true` / back visible and the label updates;
`errs=0`; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v238` Рєњ `atlas-v239`.

## iter 297 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
First confirmed an existing-feature suspicion was already handled: **number-key/AРђЊD quiz selection and 1РђЊ4 flashcard grading
already exist** (global keydown), documented in the `?` shortcuts overlay and via inline "1РђЊ4 to answer" hints Рђћ so no work
there. Shipped instead **4 "Deeper dive" expandables** on flagship hard lessons that lacked one, across four topics
(deep-dives 61 Рєњ **65**):
- **a-np-completeness** Рђћ why NP-complete problems stand or fall together: NP = *verifiable* fast (not solvable), and a
  polynomial reduction maps every NP problem onto any NP-complete one, so one fast algorithm would crack them all (P = NP).
- **c-implicit-related-rates** Рђћ related rates are the chain rule wearing a clock: differentiate a constraint w.r.t. the
  hidden variable (time) and each quantity contributes its rate, so the ladder's `2x x' + 2y y' = 0` links the two speeds.
- **l-prompting-and-in-context-learning** Рђћ in-context learning isn't learning: no weights change; the prompt *conditions*
  a frozen model, and chain-of-thought works by buying forward-pass compute + a scratchpad.
- **ps-hypothesis-testing-logic** Рђћ testing as proof by contradiction with a dial: assume `HРѓђ`, reject if the data are too
  surprising (p < ╬▒); you never *prove* HРѓђ, and ╬▒ is the false-alarm rate you accept.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each dive renders (KaTeX 0 / 13 / 0 / 9 spans Рђћ NP & ICL are
intentionally prose) with **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change.
SW cache `atlas-v237` Рєњ `atlas-v238`.

## iter 296 Рђћ Student's-t vs. normal visualizer Рђћ 64th widget (visualizations)
`ps-t-tests` had no widget, and the t-distribution's fatter tails are exactly *why* small-sample tests differ from
z-tests. Added the **64th Lab widget `ps-t-dist`**, embedded in that lesson: it overlays the **t-distribution** (violet) on
the **standard normal** (dashed), shades the `|t| > 2` tails, and shows the live tail probability **`P(t > 2)`**. Slide the
**degrees of freedom** (1РђЊ40) and the t-curve rises while its fat tails pull in Рђћ `P(t>2)` falls **14% (df 1) Рєњ 5.2%
(df 5) Рєњ ~2.5% (df 40)**, converging to the normal's 2.3% by about df = 30. The PDF is **numerically normalized** (no gamma
function needed Рђћ integrate the `(1+t┬▓/╬й)^(Рѕњ(╬й+1)/2)` shape over a wide range). Plain-unicode note (no `$`, viz-note
landmine). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 63 Рєњ 64.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**64 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed the peak rises `0.346 Рєњ 0.396 Рєњ
0.399` and tail mass shrinks `0.118 Рєњ 0.028 Рєњ 0.023` toward the normal as df grows; **via `--dump-dom`** the df slider drives
`P(t>2)` `14% Рєњ 5.2% Рєњ 2.x%`, `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0
(12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v236` Рєњ `atlas-v237`.

## iter 294 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
Pivoting off the two-iteration mobile pass back to content: a **3rd worked example** on three flagship lessons at two, each
a new technique across calc/PS/DL (examples 304 Рєњ **307**):
- **c-fundamental-theorem** Рђћ the **average value of a function**: `f╠ё = (1/(bРѕњa))РѕФf`; for `x┬▓` on `[0,3]`, the integral is 9
  Рєњ average **3**, the height of the equal-area rectangle (existing examples were a definite integral + differentiating an integral).
- **ps-conditional-independence-bayes** Рђћ the **Monty Hall problem**: the host's *non-random* reveal funnels the 2/3 onto the
  one unopened door, so switching wins **2/3** vs 1/3 Рђћ the 100-door intuition pump (existing: rare-disease base rates + two-coins).
- **dl-loss-functions** Рђћ **binary cross-entropy**: confident-right `Рѕњln(0.9)РЅѕ0.105`, hedging `Рѕњln(0.5)РЅѕ0.693`, confident-wrong
  `Рѕњln(0.1)РЅѕ2.303` Рђћ loss explodes as `pРєњ0`, punishing false confidence (existing: 3-class softmax CE + MSE).
Every value node-verified (avg = 3; BCE 0.105 / 0.693 / 2.303); injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` +
`<strong>`/`<em>` tag-balance pre-guards.

## iter 293 Рђћ Fix: Progress page overflowed horizontally on mobile (mobile / bug)
Continuing the mobile re-audit started in 292: a 390px overflow sweep across 14 key views found **one offender** Рђћ the
**Progress page (`#/stats`) scrolled horizontally** (scrollWidth 539 > 390). Traced to the **mastery-distribution strip**
(`.dist-strip`), a `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)` Рђћ five fixed columns whose content (a 30px number + 16px padding)
can't shrink below ~88px each, so five never fit in 390px. Fix: `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(88px, 1fr))` Рђћ the row wraps to
2 rows on a phone and stays a single row of 5 on desktop (within its 700px max-width). CSS-only.
Verified (iframe @ 390px + `postMessage`): `#/stats` now `sw=390, overflow=false`, the 5 cells in **2 rows**; at 760px
they're a **single row of 5** (desktop unchanged); the full 14-view 390px audit returns **over=NONE** (was `#/stats(539)`);
gate ALL GREEN; desktop smoke **errs=0/kErr=0**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v233` Рєњ `atlas-v234`.

## iter 292 Рђћ Fix: visualizations were squished on mobile (mobile / bug)
A real, longstanding mobile bug found by re-measuring the recent widgets at 390px: `.viz-canvas` had `max-width: 100%`
(so the **width** shrank to fit a narrow screen) but `canvas()` also set a **fixed inline `height`** (e.g. 350px), so the
height *didn't* shrink Рђћ every one of the 63 widgets rendered **squished horizontally** on phones (a 540├Ќ350 drawing
crammed into ~316├Ќ350, ratio 0.90 instead of 1.54 Рђћ circles became tall ellipses, labels compressed). Fix: stop setting the
inline height in `canvas()` and add `height: auto` to `.viz-canvas`, so the canvas scales **proportionally** from its own
intrinsic width:height attribute ratio. The pointer/drag helper already maps via `getBoundingClientRect()`, so dragging
stays correct at any display size Рђћ no widget logic changed.
Verified (iframe @ 390px + `postMessage`, the mobile gate): the scaling and master-theorem widgets now render **316├Ќ205,
ratio 1.54** (was 316├Ќ350, ratio 0.90); **desktop is unchanged at 540├Ќ350**; gate ALL GREEN; all-routes smoke
**errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)** including several Lab widgets + a viz-embedding lesson. One-line CSS + one-line JS change,
fixes all **63** visualizations on mobile. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v232` Рєњ `atlas-v233`.

## iter 291 Рђћ Scaling-laws visualizer Рђћ 63rd widget (visualizations)
`l-scaling-laws` had no widget, and scaling laws are one of the most abstract Рђћ and most consequential Рђћ ideas in the
LLM course. Added the **63rd Lab widget `llm-scaling`**, embedded in that lesson: for a fixed compute budget `C = 6ND`,
it plots **loss vs. model size** as a **U-curve** (Chinchilla-style `L = E + A/NрхЃ + B/DрхЃ`) and marks the **compute-optimal
size N\*** at the dip. Slide the compute budget (10┬╣РЂИРђЊ10┬▓РЂ┤ FLOPs) and `N*` marches up the curve Рђћ `91M Рєњ 2.9B Рєњ 91B` Рђћ while
the optimum stays at **~20 tokens per parameter** (the famous Chinchilla rule; constants tuned so `(B/A)^(1/a) РЅѕ 20`,
giving a constant optimal ratio). The note names `N*`, `D*`, the tokens/param ratio, and the predicted loss live Рђћ the
visceral "bigger isn't better at fixed compute; you'd starve it of tokens" lesson. Plain-unicode note + unicode-superscript
axis labels (no `$`, viz-note landmine). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 62 Рєњ 63.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**63 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** prototype confirmed the U-curve, ~21 tokens/param
constant across budgets, and N\* growing ~РѕџC with loss dropping along a power law; **via `--dump-dom`** the slider grows
`N*` `91M Рєњ 2.9B Рєњ 91.2B` across `10┬╣РЂИ/10┬▓┬╣/10┬▓РЂ┤`, `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke
**errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v231` Рєњ `atlas-v232`.

## iter 289 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Back to the owner's depth directive with **4 "Deeper dive" expandables** on flagship hard lessons that lacked one,
spread across four topics (deep-dives 57 Рєњ **61**; RL gets one for the first time since iter 277):
- **dl-rnn-lstm-gru** Рђћ why LSTMs remember and vanilla RNNs forget: the gradient picks up a factor of $W^{\top}$ at every
  step (so it scales like $\|W\|^{T}$ and vanishes/explodes), while the LSTM's *additive* cell-state highway has gradient 1
  Рђћ the same trick residual connections later reused.
- **c-intro-differential-equations** Рђћ an ODE as a *slope field*: $y'=f(x,y)$ prescribes the slope at every point, a solution
  threads tangent to it, and the initial condition just says where to drop your pencil (why a family of solutions, why IC picks one).
- **a-backtracking-branch-bound** Рђћ backtracking as DFS over *partial* solutions that prunes the instant a partial can't
  work; branch-and-bound adds a numeric bound to prune branches that can't beat the best-so-far.
- **rl-exploration** Рђћ exploration as the price of information: $\varepsilon$-greedy explores bluntly, UCB adds an optimism
  bonus $\sqrt{2\ln t / N(a)}$ so uncertainty itself draws exploration and fades as estimates firm up.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each dive renders with its summary, KaTeX typesets
(4 / 4 / 0 / 5 spans Рђћ backtracking is intentionally prose) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0** on all four; all-routes smoke
**errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v229` Рєњ `atlas-v230`.

## iter 285 Рђћ Master Theorem visualizer Рђћ 62nd widget (visualizations)
`a-recurrences-master-theorem` ("Recurrences and the Master Theorem") had **no widget** Рђћ and the master theorem is one of
the most abstract results in the algorithms course. Added the **62nd Lab widget `algo-master-theorem`**, embedded in that
lesson: a bar chart of the **total work at each level** of the recursion tree for `T(n) = a┬иT(n/b) + nрхѕ`. Since per-level
work is geometric with ratio **r = a/bрхѕ**, the bar profile *is* the case Рђћ sliders for **a, b, d** tip it root-heavy
(r<1 Рєњ ╬ў(nрхѕ)), flat (r=1 Рєњ ╬ў(nрхѕ log n)), or leaf-heavy (r>1 Рєњ ╬ў(n^log_b a)), with the dominant level highlighted and the
resulting ╬ў(┬и) named live. Presets: Merge sort (2,2,1), Strassen (7,2,2), Binary search (1,2,0). Plain-unicode note (no
`$`, viz-note landmine). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 61 Рєњ 62.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**62 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** logРѓѓ7 = 2.81; **via `--dump-dom`** the presets give the
right verdicts Рђћ Merge `r=2/2=1.00 balanced`, Strassen `r=7/4=1.75 leaf-heavy` (Рєњ ╬ў(n^2.81)), Binary search
`r=1/1=1.00 balanced`; `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**.
No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v225` Рєњ `atlas-v226`.

## iter 284 Рђћ Three more worked examples on flagship hard lessons (examples)
First, a quality check: confirmed MCQ **answer positions are well-balanced** (0 lessons exceed the 70%-at-one-index
threshold) Рђћ quizzes aren't gameable by pattern-matching. For the ship, a fresh *content type* (worked examples, last
271): added a **3rd worked example** to three flagship lessons at two, each a new technique across DL/PS/algorithms
(examples 301 Рєњ **304**):
- **dl-backpropagation** Рђћ backprop through a *two-step* chain `y=(wx+b)┬│` (existing examples were single-weight): forward
  caches `z=3, y=27`; backward `Рѕѓy/Рѕѓz┬иРѕѓz/Рѕѓw = 27┬и2 = 54`.
- **ps-confidence-intervals** Рђћ a 95% CI for a *proportion* (existing were for a mean): `p╠ѓ=0.6, n=100` Рєњ SEРЅѕ0.049,
  marginРЅѕ0.096 Рєњ `[0.504, 0.696]`.
- **a-greedy** Рђћ *fractional* knapsack, where greedy *is* optimal (existing: activity-selection + coin-change): sort by
  value density, take densest first, fraction the last Рєњ **240** Рђћ and why 0/1 (all-or-nothing) breaks greedy.
Every value node-verified; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$` + `<strong>`/`<em>` tag-balance pre-guards.

solution reveals with KaTeX (26 / 45 / 131 spans) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0**, and the answer (54 / 0.696 / 240) appears;
all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v224` Рєњ `atlas-v225`.

## iter 283 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Continuing the owner's depth directive, added **4 "Deeper dive" expandables** on flagship hard lessons that lacked one,
across four topics (deep-dives 53 Рєњ **57**):
- **a-string-algorithms** Рђћ KMP never re-reads the text: a failure function on the pattern lets it skip ahead on a
  mismatch, turning the naive $O(nm)$ into $O(n+m)$.
- **l-hallucination-and-evaluation** Рђћ hallucination is what fluency optimizes for: the next-token objective rewards
  *plausible*, never *true*, and there's no built-in "I don't know."
- **ps-point-estimation** Рђћ why sample variance divides by $n-1$: deviations from the sample mean run small (it
  minimizes them), so Bessel's correction restores an unbiased estimator ($\mathbb{E}[s^2]=\sigma^2$).
- **dl-diffusion-models** Рђћ a diffusion model just learns to denoise: a fixed forward corruption, a learned one-step
  reverse; generation = denoise repeatedly from pure noise.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with round-trip + even-`$`/no-`**`/tag-balance pre-guards.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each dive renders with its summary, KaTeX typesets
(5 / 0 / 12 / 0 spans Рђћ hallucination + diffusion are intentionally prose) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0** on all four (content
re-read to confirm integrity); all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache
`atlas-v223` Рєњ `atlas-v224`.

## iter 282 Рђћ Law of Large Numbers visualizer Рђћ 61st widget (visualizations)
`ps-law-of-large-numbers` had a deeper-dive but **no widget**, and the LLN is one of the most fundamental Рђћ and most
mis-felt Рђћ ideas in probability. Added the **61st Lab widget `ps-lln`**, embedded in that lesson: sample **a fair die**
(or a coin) and the **running average** is plotted as the count climbs Рђћ wandering wildly early, then **settling onto the
true mean** (╬╝ = 3.5 for the die), staying inside a shaded **┬▒2¤Ѓ/Рѕџn band** that visibly narrows. Controls: **Play** (rAF
animation), **+ 100** (a synchronous batch), **Reset**, and **Fair die / Coin** sources Рђћ so you watch convergence happen
for two different distributions. Distinct from the CLT viz (which shows the sample-mean *distribution*); this shows a
single average *converging*. Plain-unicode note (no `$`, viz-note landmine). app.js `viz-complete` fallback 60 Рєњ 61.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**61 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** Рђћ 400 die rolls average to ~3.41 (within 0.25 of 3.5);
**via `--dump-dom`** four "+ 100" clicks bring n to 400 with a running average of ~3.38 (converging to 3.5), `rawDollar=0`,
`errs=0`, lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache
`atlas-v222` Рєњ `atlas-v223`.

## iter 281 Рђћ Three interactive code exercises for the thinnest topics (new functionality / active learning)
Code exercises (write Рєњ **Run** Рєњ instant РюЊ/РюЌ vs expected, +15 XP on first solve) are top-tier active learning but were
lopsided: **deep learning had only 1** despite being the biggest topic, with RL next-thinnest. Added **3 gate-verified
JavaScript exercises** (lessons-with-code 25 Рєњ 28; the gate now runs **23** each build, up from 20):
- **dl-activation-functions** Рђћ a neuron's forward pass: weighted sum + bias Рєњ **ReLU**; one neuron's pre-activation is
  negative (Рєњ 0.00), the other survives (Рєњ 1.70).
- **dl-gradient-descent-and-optimizers** Рђћ one **gradient-descent step** `x Рєљ x Рѕњ lr┬и2x` on `f(x)=x┬▓`, iterated three
  times from 5 Рєњ **2.56**, sliding toward the minimum.
- **rl-mdp-formalism** Рђћ the **discounted return** `G = ╬Б ╬│рхЈrРѓќ` via Horner's rule: `[1,0,0,1]` at ╬│=0.9 Рєњ `1 + 0.9┬│` = **1.729**.
Every output node-verified before authoring; injected byte-stably with a round-trip guard and the injector refusing any
lesson that already has `data-code`.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**23 code-exercises verified** Рђћ every new output matches its `data-expected`); **via

## iter 279 Рђћ Focus timer: optional time-boxed study sessions (new functionality)
The site's whole premise is "studying alongside a full-time job," where time is scarce and a focused block beats a vague
intention Рђћ but there was no study-session tool (new-functionality was the stalest area, last 264). Added a **focus
timer**: launch a **25- or 50-minute** session from the command palette (РїўK Рєњ "focus"), and a small **persistent
countdown pill** appears in the corner (РЈ│ MM:SS ┬и pause ┬и stop). It's a fixed element + a module-level interval, so it
**rides along as you navigate** lessons. On completion it plays a **gentle three-note Web-Audio chime** (the AudioContext
is created on the start click so the delayed chime is allowed to play), **flashes the browser-tab title** ("РюЊ Focus
complete" Рђћ so it reaches you even in a background tab), and toasts the result. No XP for time (ungameable by design) Рђћ
just a calm nudge. Transient state only Рђћ **no save-shape change**; hidden in print.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** driving the real flow Рђћ РїўK Рєњ "focus" surfaces both commands at the top;
clicking the 25-min one shows the pill at **25:00**, which ticks to **24:59**; the pause button toggles to РќХ; the stop
button removes the pill; `errs=0`; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. (Completion chime/toast is the trivial
`remainingРЅц0 Рєњ stop(done)` branch; not headless-timed at 25 min.) SW cache `atlas-v219` Рєњ `atlas-v220`.

## iter 278 Рђћ SVD "rotate ┬и stretch ┬и rotate" visualizer Рђћ 60th widget (visualizations)
`la-svd` ("The Singular Value Decomposition") had a deeper-dive ("the SVD as rotate-stretch-rotate") but **no widget** Рђћ
and the SVD is the most geometric idea in linear algebra. Added the **60th Lab widget `la-svd`**, embedded in that lesson:
the unit circle and standard basis are pushed through **A = U╬БVрхђ** in four steppable stages Рђћ РЉа the circle, РЉА after the
**Vрхђ** rotation (the shape is unchanged but the colored tick-dots and basis spin), РЉб after the **╬Б** stretch (the circle
becomes an **axis-aligned ellipse** with semi-axes ¤ЃРѓЂ, ¤ЃРѓѓ), РЉБ after the **U** rotation (the final tilted ellipse). Four
sliders set the two rotation angles and the two singular values; the note shows that the ellipse's semi-axes *are* the
singular values and that `det A = ┬▒¤ЃРѓЂ¤ЃРѓѓ` is the area-scaling factor. Note is **plain unicode** (no `$`, viz-note landmine).
app.js `viz-complete` fallback 59 Рєњ 60.
**Also:** re-audited the recent additions at **390px** (the new viz + dashboard) Рђћ `docOver=0` everywhere, no mobile
regression after 12 iters of UI changes.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**60 widgets**, embed resolves); **node** Рђћ the constructed `A = U╬БVрхђ` (35┬░, ¤Ѓ=2.4/1.0, 20┬░)
has singular values exactly **2.400, 1.000** and `det = 2.400` (a genuine SVD, not a fudge); **via `--dump-dom`** stage
buttons switch the note correctly, `rawDollar=0`, `errs=0`, the lesson embed hydrates; all-routes smoke
**errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v218` Рєњ `atlas-v219`.

## iter 277 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Continuing the owner's depth directive, added **4 "Deeper dive" expandables** on flagship hard lessons that lacked one,
across four topics (deep-dives 49 Рєњ **53**):
- **dl-attention-mechanism** Рђћ attention as a *soft, differentiable dictionary lookup*: query┬иkey Рєњ softmax Рєњ weighted
  values; content-addressable memory made continuous (scaled by $1/\sqrt{d_k}$).
- **c-improper-integrals** Рђћ an improper integral as a *limit in disguise*: $\int_1^{\infty}=\lim_{b\to\infty}\int_1^{b}$;
  $\int_1^{\infty}x^{-p}$ converges iff $p>1$ ($1/x^2$ converges, $1/x$ diverges).
- **rl-actor-critic** Рђћ actor-critic as *REINFORCE with a baseline*: act on the advantage $A=G_t-V(s)$ Рђћ same expectation
  (no bias), far less variance.
- **la-inverse-and-systems** Рђћ why you *almost never* form $A^{-1}$: solving $A\mathbf{x}=\mathbf{b}$ by $LU$/elimination
  is ~3├Ќ cheaper and numerically safer; the inverse is the right mental model, the wrong computation.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with a round-trip guard + even-`$`, no-`**`, and tag-balance
pre-guards (incl. `<code>`).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each dive renders with its summary, KaTeX typesets
(1 / 7 / 3 / 11 spans) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0** on all four; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No
save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v217` Рєњ `atlas-v218`.

## iter 276 Рђћ "Best study day yet!" Рђћ completing the personal-bests celebration trio (gamification)
The Personal Bests panel tracks three lifetime records Рђћ longest streak, best test score, and **biggest single-day XP**.
Two of the three already celebrate *in the moment* (­ЪЈє record streak, ­Ъј» best test), but beating your best-ever day was
shown only statically. Added the missing **"РџА Best study day yet!"** toast: the instant today's running XP total crosses
your previous best-ever day, it fires once. Detected cleanly inside `addXP` by catching the *crossing* (today's total was
РЅц the prior best before this gain, and exceeds it after) Рђћ so it fires **exactly once per day, never on your very first
active day** (no prior record to beat), and needs **no new persisted state** (a transient `_bestDaySet` signal, drained by
the UI in `flushAchievements()` right beside the existing goal/streak toasts).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **node test** Рђћ a save with a prior best of 200 stays quiet at today=150, fires `230` when a
gain crosses to 230, then stays quiet on further same-day gains, and a first-ever active day never fires; **in-browser**
completing a lesson (today 0Рєњ50, prior best 30) raises "РџА Best study day yet! 50 XP today Рђћ a new single-day record"
alongside the normal completion toasts; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache
`atlas-v216` Рєњ `atlas-v217`.

## iter 275 Рђћ Screen-reader fixes: live quiz feedback + labeled test selects (accessibility)
An a11y pass (the stalest area, last 263) caught two real gaps. **(1) Quiz feedback was silent to screen readers.** When
you answer a question, "Correct РюЊ / Not quite" plus the explanation is injected into a slot Рђћ but those slots weren't
live regions, so a screen-reader user heard *nothing* and had to go hunting for the result. Added `aria-live="polite"` to
all three instant-feedback slots: the per-lesson **Quiz** (`#explain-slot`), the inline **Quick Check**
(`.qc-explain-slot`), and the **Daily-Mix / mastery drill** (`#md-explain`) Рђћ so the verdict and explanation are now
announced the moment they appear. **(2) The test-config selects were unlabeled.** The "Scope" and "Length" `<label>`s sat
next to `#t-scope` / `#t-len` with no `for=`, so they weren't programmatically associated Рђћ a screen reader announced the
dropdowns with no name. Added `for="t-scope"` / `for="t-len"` (which also makes the label text click-to-focus the select).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; **via `--dump-dom`** Рђћ answering a quiz question leaves `#explain-slot` with
`aria-live="polite"` and the "Correct/Not quite" text inside it; on `#/test` both `label[for="t-scope"]` and
`label[for="t-len"]` resolve to their selects; all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW
cache `atlas-v215` Рєњ `atlas-v216`.

## iter 274 Рђћ Statistical-power visualizer Рђћ 59th widget (visualizations)
Probability & Statistics was the thinnest viz topic (6), and `ps-errors-and-power` ("Type I & II Errors and Statistical
Power") Рђћ one of the most-misunderstood topics in stats Рђћ had **no widget**. Added the **59th Lab widget `ps-power`**,
embedded in that lesson: two overlapping normal curves, the null **HРѓђ** (mean 0) and an alternative **HРѓЂ** (mean = effect
size *d*), with a draggable **decision threshold**. The shaded tails make the whole logic concrete Рђћ **╬▒** (Type I /
false positive: HРѓђ right of the threshold, rust), **╬▓** (Type II / miss: HРѓЂ left of it, gold), and **power = 1 Рѕњ ╬▓** (HРѓЂ
right of it, sage) Рђћ with live numbers. Two sliders (threshold, effect size) and presets (╬▒=0.05, large effect,
underpowered) let you *feel* the trade-offs: move the threshold right Рєњ ╬▒ shrinks but ╬▓ grows; push the curves apart Рєњ
power climbs. Uses an erf-based ╬д; note is **plain text** (no `$`, per the viz-note landmine). app.js `viz-complete`
fallback 58 Рєњ 59.
Verified: gate ALL GREEN (**59 widgets**, embed resolves); **via `--dump-dom`** the note computes correctly Рђћ at
threshold z=1.65, d=2: **╬▒=0.050 ┬и ╬▓=0.361 ┬и power=0.639** (and rawDollar=0, confirming no un-rendered `$`); sliders +
presets update it; the lesson embed hydrates (canvas present); all-routes smoke **errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No
save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v214` Рєњ `atlas-v215`.

## iter 273 Рђћ Four more deeper-dives on flagship hard lessons (content / understandability)
Continuing the owner's depth directive, added **4 "Deeper dive" expandables** on flagship hard lessons that lacked one,
spread across four topics 265 didn't deepen (deep-dives 45 Рєњ **49**):
- **dl-gans** Рђћ a GAN is a two-player <em>minimax game</em>, not an optimization; the target is a Nash equilibrium, which
  is why training oscillates and mode-collapses.
- **ps-poisson** Рђћ the Poisson distribution as the <em>law of rare events</em>: the binomial limit as $n\to\infty$,
  $p\to 0$, $np=\lambda$ fixed; one parameter is both mean and variance.
- **a-mst-union-find** Рђћ Kruskal's MST as greed made safe by the <em>cut property</em>, with Union-Find as the
  near-constant-time cycle test (pure prose, no math).
- **l-peft-lora** Рђћ LoRA as a low-rank patch $\Delta W = BA$ ($r \ll d$): why fine-tuning needs well under 1% of the
  weights, and why $W$ stays exact.
Authored with `String.raw` LaTeX; injected byte-stably with a round-trip guard + even-`$`, no-`**`, and `<details>`/
`<b>`/`<em>` tag-balance pre-guards (now also caught by the gate).
Verified: gate ALL GREEN; per-lesson **via `--dump-dom`** each dive renders with its summary, KaTeX typesets
(9 / 11 / 0 / 22 spans Рђћ MST is intentionally prose) and **kErr=0, rawDollar=0** on all four; all-routes smoke
**errs=0/kErr=0 (12 routes)**. No save-shape change. SW cache `atlas-v213` Рєњ `atlas-v214`.

(`.palette-scrim` present), `errs=0`; **desktop 1200px** Рђћ it computes `display: none` (no desktop redundancy/clutter);
